5008 SET_QNT(qnt_Cho2, "Cho2", "HO2 volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5009 SET_QNT(qnt_Ch2o2, "Ch2o2", "H2O2 volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5010 SET_QNT(qnt_Co1d, "Co1d", "O(1D) volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5011 SET_QNT(qnt_Co3p, "Co3p", "O(3P) radical volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5012 SET_QNT(qnt_Cccl4, "Cccl4", "CCl4 (CFC-10) volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5013 SET_QNT(qnt_Cccl3f, "Cccl3f", "CCl3F (CFC-11) volume mixing ratio",
5014 "ppv")
5015 SET_QNT(qnt_Cccl2f2, "Cccl2f2", "CCl2F2 (CFC-12) volume mixing ratio",
5016 "ppv")
5017 SET_QNT(qnt_Cn2o, "Cn2o", "N2O volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5018 SET_QNT(qnt_Csf6, "Csf6", "SF6 volume mixing ratio", "ppv")
5019 SET_QNT(qnt_aoa, "aoa", "age of air", "s")
5020 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "QNT_UNIT", iq, "", ctl->qnt_unit[iq]);
5021 }
5022
5023 /* Vertical coordinates and velocities... */
5024 ctl->advect_vert_coord =
5025 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ADVECT_VERT_COORD", -1, "0", NULL);
5026 if (ctl->advect_vert_coord < 0 || ctl->advect_vert_coord > 2)
5027 ERRMSG("Set advect_vert_coord to 0, 1, or 2!");
5028 if (ctl->advect_vert_coord == 1 && ctl->qnt_zeta < 0)
5029 ERRMSG("Please add zeta to your quantities for diabatic calculations!");
5030 ctl->met_vert_coord =
5031 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_VERT_COORD", -1, "0", NULL);
5032 if (ctl->advect_vert_coord == 2 && ctl->met_vert_coord != 1)
5033 ERRMSG
5034 ("Using ADVECT_VERT_COORD = 2 requires meteo data on model levels!");
5035 ctl->met_clams =
5036 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CLAMS", -1, "0", NULL);
5037 ctl->advect_cpl_zeta_and_press_modules =
5038 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ADVECT_ZETA_PRESS_MODULES", -1, "1",
5039 NULL);
5040
5041 /* Time steps of simulation... */
5042 ctl->direction =
5043 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "DIRECTION", -1, "1", NULL);
5044 if (ctl->direction != -1 && ctl->direction != 1)
5045 ERRMSG("Set DIRECTION to -1 or 1!");
5046 ctl->t_stop = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "T_STOP", -1, "1e100", NULL);
5047 ctl->dt_mod = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "DT_MOD", -1, "180", NULL);
5048
5049 /* Meteo data... */
5050 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "METBASE", -1, "-", ctl->metbase);
5051 ctl->dt_met = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "DT_MET", -1, "3600", NULL);
5052 ctl->met_convention =
5053 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CONVENTION", -1, "0", NULL);
5054 ctl->met_type =
5055 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_TYPE", -1, "0", NULL);
5056 if (ctl->advect_vert_coord == 1 && ctl->met_type != 0)
5057 ERRMSG
5058 ("Please use meteorological files in netcdf format for diabatic calculations.");
5059 ctl->met_nc_scale =
5060 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_NC_SCALE", -1, "1", NULL);
5061 ctl->met_nc_level =
5062 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_NC_LEVEL", -1, "0", NULL);
5063 ctl->met_nc_quant =
5064 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_NC_QUANT", -1, "0", NULL);
5065 ctl->met_zfp_prec =
5066 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_ZFP_PREC", -1, "8", NULL);
5067 ctl->met_zfp_tol_t =
5068 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_ZFP_TOL_T", -1, "5.0", NULL);
5069 ctl->met_zfp_tol_z =
5070 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_ZFP_TOL_Z", -1, "0.5", NULL);
5071 ctl->met_cms_batch =
5072 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_BATCH", -1, "-1", NULL);
5073 ctl->met_cms_heur =
5074 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_HEUR", -1, "1", NULL);
5075 ctl->met_cms_eps_z =
5076 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_Z", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5077 ctl->met_cms_eps_t =
5078 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_T", -1, "0.05", NULL);
5079 ctl->met_cms_eps_u =
5080 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_U", -1, "0.05", NULL);
5081 ctl->met_cms_eps_v =
5082 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_V", -1, "0.05", NULL);
5083 ctl->met_cms_eps_w =
5084 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_W", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5085 ctl->met_cms_eps_pv =
5086 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_PV", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5087 ctl->met_cms_eps_h2o =
5088 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_H2O", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5089 ctl->met_cms_eps_o3 =
5090 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_O3", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5091 ctl->met_cms_eps_lwc =
5092 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_LWC", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5093 ctl->met_cms_eps_rwc =
5094 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_RWC", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5095 ctl->met_cms_eps_iwc =
5096 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_IWC", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5097 ctl->met_cms_eps_swc =
5098 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_SWC", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5099 ctl->met_cms_eps_cc =
5100 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CMS_EPS_CC", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5101 ctl->met_dx = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_DX", -1, "1", NULL);
5102 ctl->met_dy = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_DY", -1, "1", NULL);
5103 ctl->met_dp = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_DP", -1, "1", NULL);
5104 if (ctl->met_dx < 1 || ctl->met_dy < 1 || ctl->met_dp < 1)
5105 ERRMSG("MET_DX, MET_DY, and MET_DP need to be greater than zero!");
5106 ctl->met_sx = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_SX", -1, "1", NULL);
5107 ctl->met_sy = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_SY", -1, "1", NULL);
5108 ctl->met_sp = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_SP", -1, "1", NULL);
5109 if (ctl->met_sx < 1 || ctl->met_sy < 1 || ctl->met_sp < 1)
5110 ERRMSG("MET_SX, MET_SY, and MET_SP need to be greater than zero!");
5111 ctl->met_detrend =
5112 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_DETREND", -1, "-999", NULL);
5113 ctl->met_np = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_NP", -1, "0", NULL);
5114 if (ctl->met_np > EP)
5115 ERRMSG("Too many levels!");
5116 ctl->met_press_level_def =
5117 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_PRESS_LEVEL_DEF", -1, "-1",
5118 NULL);
5119 if (ctl->met_press_level_def >= 0) {
5120 level_definitions(ctl);
5121 } else {
5122 if (ctl->met_np > 0) {
5123 for (int ip = 0; ip < ctl->met_np; ip++)
5124 ctl->met_p[ip] =
5125 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_P", ip, "", NULL);
5126 }
5127 }
5128 ctl->met_geopot_sx
5129 = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_GEOPOT_SX", -1, "-1", NULL);
5130 ctl->met_geopot_sy
5131 = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_GEOPOT_SY", -1, "-1", NULL);
5132 ctl->met_relhum
5133 = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_RELHUM", -1, "0", NULL);
5134 ctl->met_tropo =
5135 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_TROPO", -1, "3", NULL);
5136 if (ctl->met_tropo < 0 || ctl->met_tropo > 5)
5137 ERRMSG("Set MET_TROPO = 0 ... 5!");
5138 ctl->met_tropo_pv =
5139 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_TROPO_PV", -1, "3.5", NULL);
5140 ctl->met_tropo_theta =
5141 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_TROPO_THETA", -1, "380", NULL);
5142 ctl->met_tropo_spline =
5143 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_TROPO_SPLINE", -1, "1", NULL);
5144 ctl->met_dt_out =
5145 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_DT_OUT", -1, "0.1", NULL);
5146 ctl->met_cache =
5147 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_CACHE", -1, "0", NULL);
5148 ctl->met_mpi_share =
5149 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MET_MPI_SHARE", -1, "0", NULL);
5150
5151 /* Sorting... */
5152 ctl->sort_dt = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SORT_DT", -1, "-999", NULL);
5153
5154 /* Isosurface parameters... */
5155 ctl->isosurf =
5156 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ISOSURF", -1, "0", NULL);
5157 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BALLOON", -1, "-", ctl->balloon);
5158
5159 /* Random number generator... */
5160 ctl->rng_type =
5161 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "RNG_TYPE", -1, "0", NULL);
5162 if (ctl->rng_type < 0 || ctl->rng_type > 2)
5163 ERRMSG("Set RNG_TYPE to 0, 1, or 2!");
5164
5165 /* Advection parameters... */
5166 ctl->advect = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ADVECT", -1, "2", NULL);
5167 if (!(ctl->advect == 0 || ctl->advect == 1
5168 || ctl->advect == 2 || ctl->advect == 4))
5169 ERRMSG("Set ADVECT to 0, 1, 2, or 4!");
5170 ctl->reflect =
5171 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "REFLECT", -1, "0", NULL);
5172
5173 /* Diffusion parameters... */
5174 ctl->turb_dx_trop =
5175 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TURB_DX_TROP", -1, "50", NULL);
5176 ctl->turb_dx_strat =
5177 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TURB_DX_STRAT", -1, "0", NULL);
5178 ctl->turb_dz_trop =
5179 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TURB_DZ_TROP", -1, "0", NULL);
5180 ctl->turb_dz_strat =
5181 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TURB_DZ_STRAT", -1, "0.1", NULL);
5182 ctl->turb_mesox =
5183 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TURB_MESOX", -1, "0.16", NULL);
5184 ctl->turb_mesoz =
5185 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TURB_MESOZ", -1, "0.16", NULL);
5186
5187 /* Convection... */
5188 ctl->conv_cape
5189 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CONV_CAPE", -1, "-999", NULL);
5190 ctl->conv_cin
5191 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CONV_CIN", -1, "-999", NULL);
5192 ctl->conv_dt = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CONV_DT", -1, "-999", NULL);
5193 ctl->conv_mix_bot
5194 = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CONV_MIX_BOT", -1, "1", NULL);
5195 ctl->conv_mix_top
5196 = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CONV_MIX_TOP", -1, "1", NULL);
5197
5198 /* Boundary conditions... */
5199 ctl->bound_mass =
5200 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_MASS", -1, "-999", NULL);
5201 ctl->bound_mass_trend =
5202 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_MASS_TREND", -1, "0", NULL);
5203 ctl->bound_vmr =
5204 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_VMR", -1, "-999", NULL);
5205 ctl->bound_vmr_trend =
5206 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_VMR_TREND", -1, "0", NULL);
5207 ctl->bound_lat0 =
5208 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_LAT0", -1, "-999", NULL);
5209 ctl->bound_lat1 =
5210 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_LAT1", -1, "-999", NULL);
5211 ctl->bound_p0 =
5212 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_P0", -1, "-999", NULL);
5213 ctl->bound_p1 =
5214 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_P1", -1, "-999", NULL);
5215 ctl->bound_dps =
5216 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_DPS", -1, "-999", NULL);
5217 ctl->bound_dzs =
5218 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_DZS", -1, "-999", NULL);
5219 ctl->bound_zetas =
5220 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_ZETAS", -1, "-999", NULL);
5221 ctl->bound_pbl =
5222 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "BOUND_PBL", -1, "0", NULL);
5223
5224 /* Species parameters... */
5225 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SPECIES", -1, "-", ctl->species);
5226 if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "CF2Cl2") == 0) {
5227 ctl->molmass = 120.907;
5228 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 3e-5;
5229 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 3500.0;
5230 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "CFCl3") == 0) {
5231 ctl->molmass = 137.359;
5232 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 1.1e-4;
5233 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 3300.0;
5234 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "CH4") == 0) {
5235 ctl->molmass = 16.043;
5236 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 2;
5237 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 2.45e-12;
5238 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 1775;
5239 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 1.4e-5;
5240 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 1600.0;
5241 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "CO") == 0) {
5242 ctl->molmass = 28.01;
5243 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 3;
5244 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 6.9e-33;
5245 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 2.1;
5246 ctl->oh_chem[2] = 1.1e-12;
5247 ctl->oh_chem[3] = -1.3;
5248 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 9.7e-6;
5249 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 1300.0;
5250 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "CO2") == 0) {
5251 ctl->molmass = 44.009;
5252 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 3.3e-4;
5253 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 2400.0;
5254 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "H2O") == 0) {
5255 ctl->molmass = 18.01528;
5256 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "N2O") == 0) {
5257 ctl->molmass = 44.013;
5258 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 2.4e-4;
5259 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 2600.;
5260 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "NH3") == 0) {
5261 ctl->molmass = 17.031;
5262 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 2;
5263 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 1.7e-12;
5264 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 710;
5265 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 5.9e-1;
5266 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 4200.0;
5267 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "HNO3") == 0) {
5268 ctl->molmass = 63.012;
5269 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 2.1e3;
5270 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 8700.0;
5271 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "NO") == 0) {
5272 ctl->molmass = 30.006;
5273 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 3;
5274 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 7.1e-31;
5275 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 2.6;
5276 ctl->oh_chem[2] = 3.6e-11;
5277 ctl->oh_chem[3] = 0.1;
5278 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 1.9e-5;
5279 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 1600.0;
5280 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "NO2") == 0) {
5281 ctl->molmass = 46.005;
5282 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 3;
5283 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 1.8e-30;
5284 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 3.0;
5285 ctl->oh_chem[2] = 2.8e-11;
5286 ctl->oh_chem[3] = 0.0;
5287 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 1.2e-4;
5288 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 2400.0;
5289 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "O3") == 0) {
5290 ctl->molmass = 47.997;
5291 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 2;
5292 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 1.7e-12;
5293 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 940;
5294 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 1e-4;
5295 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 2800.0;
5296 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "SF6") == 0) {
5297 ctl->molmass = 146.048;
5298 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 2.4e-6;
5299 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 3100.0;
5300 } else if (strcasecmp(ctl->species, "SO2") == 0) {
5301 ctl->molmass = 64.066;
5302 ctl->oh_chem_reaction = 3;
5303 ctl->oh_chem[0] = 2.9e-31;
5304 ctl->oh_chem[1] = 4.1;
5305 ctl->oh_chem[2] = 1.7e-12;
5306 ctl->oh_chem[3] = -0.2;
5307 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[0] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[0] = 1.3e-2;
5308 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[1] = ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[1] = 2900.0;
5309 }
5310
5311 /* Molar mass... */
5312 char defstr[LEN];
5313 sprintf(defstr, "%g", ctl->molmass);
5314 ctl->molmass = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MOLMASS", -1, defstr, NULL);
5315
5316 /* OH chemistry... */
5317 sprintf(defstr, "%d", ctl->oh_chem_reaction);
5318 ctl->oh_chem_reaction =
5319 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "OH_CHEM_REACTION", -1, defstr,
5320 NULL);
5321 for (int ip = 0; ip < 4; ip++) {
5322 sprintf(defstr, "%g", ctl->oh_chem[ip]);
5323 ctl->oh_chem[ip] =
5324 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "OH_CHEM", ip, defstr, NULL);
5325 }
5326 ctl->oh_chem_beta =
5327 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "OH_CHEM_BETA", -1, "0", NULL);
5328
5329 /* H2O2 chemistry... */
5330 ctl->h2o2_chem_reaction =
5331 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "H2O2_CHEM_REACTION", -1, "0", NULL);
5332
5333 /* KPP chemistry... */
5334 ctl->kpp_chem =
5335 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "KPP_CHEM", -1, "0", NULL);
5336 ctl->dt_kpp = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "DT_KPP", -1, "1800", NULL);
5337
5338 /* First order tracer chemistry... */
5339 ctl->tracer_chem =
5340 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TRACER_CHEM", -1, "0", NULL);
5341
5342 /* Wet deposition... */
5343 for (int ip = 0; ip < 3; ip++) {
5344 sprintf(defstr, "%g", ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[ip]);
5345 ctl->wet_depo_ic_h[ip] =
5346 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_IC_H", ip, defstr, NULL);
5347 }
5348 for (int ip = 0; ip < 1; ip++) {
5349 sprintf(defstr, "%g", ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[ip]);
5350 ctl->wet_depo_bc_h[ip] =
5351 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_BC_H", ip, defstr, NULL);
5352 }
5353 ctl->wet_depo_ic_a =
5354 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_IC_A", -1, "0", NULL);
5355 ctl->wet_depo_ic_b =
5356 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_IC_B", -1, "0", NULL);
5357 ctl->wet_depo_bc_a =
5358 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_BC_A", -1, "0", NULL);
5359 ctl->wet_depo_bc_b =
5360 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_BC_B", -1, "0", NULL);
5361 ctl->wet_depo_pre[0] =
5362 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_PRE", 0, "0.5", NULL);
5363 ctl->wet_depo_pre[1] =
5364 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_PRE", 1, "0.36", NULL);
5365 ctl->wet_depo_ic_ret_ratio =
5366 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_IC_RET_RATIO", -1, "1", NULL);
5367 ctl->wet_depo_bc_ret_ratio =
5368 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "WET_DEPO_BC_RET_RATIO", -1, "1", NULL);
5369
5370 /* Dry deposition... */
5371 ctl->dry_depo_vdep =
5372 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "DRY_DEPO_VDEP", -1, "0", NULL);
5373 ctl->dry_depo_dp =
5374 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "DRY_DEPO_DP", -1, "30", NULL);
5375
5376 /* Climatological data... */
5377 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_PHOTO", -1,
5378 "../../data/clams_photolysis_rates.nc", ctl->clim_photo);
5379 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_HNO3_FILENAME", -1,
5380 "../../data/gozcards_HNO3.nc", ctl->clim_hno3_filename);
5381 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_OH_FILENAME", -1,
5382 "../../data/clams_radical_species_vmr.nc", ctl->clim_oh_filename);
5383 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_H2O2_FILENAME", -1,
5384 "../../data/cams_H2O2.nc", ctl->clim_h2o2_filename);
5385 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_HO2_FILENAME", -1,
5386 "../../data/clams_radical_species_vmr.nc", ctl->clim_ho2_filename);
5387 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_O1D_FILENAME", -1,
5388 "../../data/clams_radical_species_vmr.nc", ctl->clim_o1d_filename);
5389 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_CCL4_TIMESERIES", -1,
5390 "../../data/noaa_gml_ccl4.tab", ctl->clim_ccl4_timeseries);
5391 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_CCL3F_TIMESERIES", -1,
5392 "../../data/noaa_gml_cfc11.tab", ctl->clim_ccl3f_timeseries);
5393 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_CCL2F2_TIMESERIES", -1,
5394 "../../data/noaa_gml_cfc12.tab", ctl->clim_ccl2f2_timeseries);
5395 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_N2O_TIMESERIES", -1,
5396 "../../data/noaa_gml_n2o.tab", ctl->clim_n2o_timeseries);
5397 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CLIM_SF6_TIMESERIES", -1,
5398 "../../data/noaa_gml_sf6.tab", ctl->clim_sf6_timeseries);
5399
5400 /* Mixing... */
5401 ctl->mixing_dt =
5402 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_DT", -1, "3600.", NULL);
5403 ctl->mixing_trop =
5404 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_TROP", -1, "-999", NULL);
5405 ctl->mixing_strat =
5406 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_STRAT", -1, "-999", NULL);
5407 ctl->mixing_z0 =
5408 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_Z0", -1, "-5", NULL);
5409 ctl->mixing_z1 =
5410 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_Z1", -1, "85", NULL);
5411 ctl->mixing_nz =
5412 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_NZ", -1, "90", NULL);
5413 ctl->mixing_lon0 =
5414 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_LON0", -1, "-180", NULL);
5415 ctl->mixing_lon1 =
5416 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_LON1", -1, "180", NULL);
5417 ctl->mixing_nx =
5418 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_NX", -1, "360", NULL);
5419 ctl->mixing_lat0 =
5420 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_LAT0", -1, "-90", NULL);
5421 ctl->mixing_lat1 =
5422 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_LAT1", -1, "90", NULL);
5423 ctl->mixing_ny =
5424 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "MIXING_NY", -1, "180", NULL);
5425
5426 /* Chemistry grid... */
5427 ctl->chemgrid_z0 =
5428 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_Z0", -1, "-5", NULL);
5429 ctl->chemgrid_z1 =
5430 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_Z1", -1, "85", NULL);
5431 ctl->chemgrid_nz =
5432 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_NZ", -1, "90", NULL);
5433 ctl->chemgrid_lon0 =
5434 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_LON0", -1, "-180", NULL);
5435 ctl->chemgrid_lon1 =
5436 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_LON1", -1, "180", NULL);
5437 ctl->chemgrid_nx =
5438 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_NX", -1, "360", NULL);
5439 ctl->chemgrid_lat0 =
5440 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_LAT0", -1, "-90", NULL);
5441 ctl->chemgrid_lat1 =
5442 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_LAT1", -1, "90", NULL);
5443 ctl->chemgrid_ny =
5444 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CHEMGRID_NY", -1, "180", NULL);
5445
5446 /* Exponential decay... */
5447 ctl->tdec_trop = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TDEC_TROP", -1, "0", NULL);
5448 ctl->tdec_strat
5449 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "TDEC_STRAT", -1, "0", NULL);
5450
5451 /* PSC analysis... */
5452 ctl->psc_h2o = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PSC_H2O", -1, "4e-6", NULL);
5453 ctl->psc_hno3 =
5454 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PSC_HNO3", -1, "9e-9", NULL);
5455
5456 /* Output of atmospheric data... */
5457 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_BASENAME", -1, "-", ctl->atm_basename);
5458 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_GPFILE", -1, "-", ctl->atm_gpfile);
5459 ctl->atm_dt_out =
5460 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_DT_OUT", -1, "86400", NULL);
5461 ctl->atm_filter =
5462 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_FILTER", -1, "0", NULL);
5463 ctl->atm_stride =
5464 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_STRIDE", -1, "1", NULL);
5465 ctl->atm_type =
5466 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_TYPE", -1, "0", NULL);
5467 ctl->atm_type_out =
5468 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_TYPE_OUT", -1, "-1", NULL);
5469 if (ctl->atm_type_out == -1)
5470 ctl->atm_type_out = ctl->atm_type;
5471 ctl->atm_nc_level =
5472 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_NC_LEVEL", -1, "0", NULL);
5473 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
5474 ctl->atm_nc_quant[iq] =
5475 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ATM_NC_QUANT", iq, "0", NULL);
5476 ctl->obs_type =
5477 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "OBS_TYPE", -1, "0", NULL);
5478
5479 /* Output of CSI data... */
5480 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_BASENAME", -1, "-", ctl->csi_basename);
5481 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_KERNEL", -1, "-", ctl->csi_kernel);
5482 ctl->csi_dt_out =
5483 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_DT_OUT", -1, "86400", NULL);
5484 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_OBSFILE", -1, "-", ctl->csi_obsfile);
5485 ctl->csi_obsmin =
5486 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_OBSMIN", -1, "0", NULL);
5487 ctl->csi_modmin =
5488 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_MODMIN", -1, "0", NULL);
5489 ctl->csi_z0 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_Z0", -1, "-5", NULL);
5490 ctl->csi_z1 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_Z1", -1, "85", NULL);
5491 ctl->csi_nz = (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_NZ", -1, "1", NULL);
5492 ctl->csi_lon0 =
5493 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_LON0", -1, "-180", NULL);
5494 ctl->csi_lon1 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_LON1", -1, "180", NULL);
5495 ctl->csi_nx =
5496 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_NX", -1, "360", NULL);
5497 ctl->csi_lat0 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_LAT0", -1, "-90", NULL);
5498 ctl->csi_lat1 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_LAT1", -1, "90", NULL);
5499 ctl->csi_ny =
5500 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "CSI_NY", -1, "180", NULL);
5501
5502 /* Output of ensemble data... */
5503 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ENS_BASENAME", -1, "-", ctl->ens_basename);
5504 ctl->ens_dt_out =
5505 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "ENS_DT_OUT", -1, "86400", NULL);
5506
5507 /* Output of grid data... */
5508 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_BASENAME", -1, "-",
5509 ctl->grid_basename);
5510 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_KERNEL", -1, "-", ctl->grid_kernel);
5511 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_GPFILE", -1, "-", ctl->grid_gpfile);
5512 ctl->grid_dt_out =
5513 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_DT_OUT", -1, "86400", NULL);
5514 ctl->grid_sparse =
5515 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_SPARSE", -1, "0", NULL);
5516 ctl->grid_nc_level =
5517 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_NC_LEVEL", -1, "0", NULL);
5518 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
5519 ctl->grid_nc_quant[iq] =
5520 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_NC_QUANT", iq, "0", NULL);
5521 ctl->grid_stddev =
5522 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_STDDEV", -1, "0", NULL);
5523 ctl->grid_z0 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_Z0", -1, "-5", NULL);
5524 ctl->grid_z1 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_Z1", -1, "85", NULL);
5525 ctl->grid_nz =
5526 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_NZ", -1, "1", NULL);
5527 ctl->grid_lon0 =
5528 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_LON0", -1, "-180", NULL);
5529 ctl->grid_lon1 =
5530 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_LON1", -1, "180", NULL);
5531 ctl->grid_nx =
5532 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_NX", -1, "360", NULL);
5533 ctl->grid_lat0 =
5534 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_LAT0", -1, "-90", NULL);
5535 ctl->grid_lat1 =
5536 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_LAT1", -1, "90", NULL);
5537 ctl->grid_ny =
5538 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_NY", -1, "180", NULL);
5539 ctl->grid_type =
5540 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "GRID_TYPE", -1, "0", NULL);
5541
5542 /* Output of profile data... */
5543 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_BASENAME", -1, "-",
5544 ctl->prof_basename);
5545 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_OBSFILE", -1, "-", ctl->prof_obsfile);
5546 ctl->prof_z0 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_Z0", -1, "0", NULL);
5547 ctl->prof_z1 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_Z1", -1, "60", NULL);
5548 ctl->prof_nz =
5549 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_NZ", -1, "60", NULL);
5550 ctl->prof_lon0 =
5551 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_LON0", -1, "-180", NULL);
5552 ctl->prof_lon1 =
5553 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_LON1", -1, "180", NULL);
5554 ctl->prof_nx =
5555 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_NX", -1, "360", NULL);
5556 ctl->prof_lat0 =
5557 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_LAT0", -1, "-90", NULL);
5558 ctl->prof_lat1 =
5559 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_LAT1", -1, "90", NULL);
5560 ctl->prof_ny =
5561 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "PROF_NY", -1, "180", NULL);
5562
5563 /* Output of sample data... */
5564 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SAMPLE_BASENAME", -1, "-",
5565 ctl->sample_basename);
5566 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SAMPLE_KERNEL", -1, "-",
5567 ctl->sample_kernel);
5568 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SAMPLE_OBSFILE", -1, "-",
5569 ctl->sample_obsfile);
5570 ctl->sample_dx =
5571 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SAMPLE_DX", -1, "50", NULL);
5572 ctl->sample_dz =
5573 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "SAMPLE_DZ", -1, "-999", NULL);
5574
5575 /* Output of station data... */
5576 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "STAT_BASENAME", -1, "-",
5577 ctl->stat_basename);
5578 ctl->stat_lon = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "STAT_LON", -1, "0", NULL);
5579 ctl->stat_lat = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "STAT_LAT", -1, "0", NULL);
5580 ctl->stat_r = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "STAT_R", -1, "50", NULL);
5581 ctl->stat_t0 =
5582 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "STAT_T0", -1, "-1e100", NULL);
5583 ctl->stat_t1 = scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "STAT_T1", -1, "1e100", NULL);
5584
5585 /* Output of VTK data... */
5586 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "VTK_BASENAME", -1, "-", ctl->vtk_basename);
5587 ctl->vtk_dt_out =
5588 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "VTK_DT_OUT", -1, "86400", NULL);
5589 ctl->vtk_stride =
5590 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "VTK_STRIDE", -1, "1", NULL);
5591 ctl->vtk_scale =
5592 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "VTK_SCALE", -1, "1.0", NULL);
5593 ctl->vtk_offset =
5594 scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "VTK_OFFSET", -1, "0.0", NULL);
5595 ctl->vtk_sphere =
5596 (int) scan_ctl(filename, argc, argv, "VTK_SPHERE", -1, "0", NULL);
5597}

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ read_kernel()

void read_kernel ( const char *  filename,
double  kz[EP],
double  kw[EP],
int *  nk 
)

Reads kernel function data from a file and populates the provided arrays.

This function reads kernel function data from a specified file, populating the provided arrays kz and kw with the parsed data. It also updates the variable pointed to by nk with the number of data points read. The function ensures that the height levels are in ascending order and performs checks for the number of height levels read.

Parameters
filenameA string containing the path to the file containing kernel function data.
kzA double array to store the height levels of the kernel function.
kwA double array to store the weights corresponding to the height levels.
nkA pointer to an integer variable representing the number of data points read.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Logs information indicating the kernel function file being read.
  • Attempts to open the specified file for reading.
  • Reads data from the file line by line, parsing height levels and weights.
  • Checks that the height levels are in ascending order and that the number of data points does not exceed the defined maximum.
  • Closes the file after reading.
  • Updates the value of nk with the number of data points read.
  • Normalizes the kernel function weights by dividing each weight by the maximum weight.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 5601 of file mptrac.c.

5605 {
5606
5607 /* Write info... */
5608 LOG(1, "Read kernel function: %s", filename);
5609
5610 /* Open file... */
5611 FILE *in;
5612 if (!(in = fopen(filename, "r")))
5613 ERRMSG("Cannot open file!");
5614
5615 /* Read data... */
5616 char line[LEN];
5617 int n = 0;
5618 while (fgets(line, LEN, in))
5619 if (sscanf(line, "%lg %lg", &kz[n], &kw[n]) == 2) {
5620 if (n > 0 && kz[n] < kz[n - 1])
5621 ERRMSG("Height levels must be ascending!");
5622 if ((++n) >= EP)
5623 ERRMSG("Too many height levels!");
5624 }
5625
5626 /* Close file... */
5627 fclose(in);
5628
5629 /* Check number of data points... */
5630 *nk = n;
5631 if (n < 2)
5632 ERRMSG("Not enough height levels!");
5633
5634 /* Normalize kernel function... */
5635 const double kmax = gsl_stats_max(kw, 1, (size_t) n);
5636 for (int iz = 0; iz < n; iz++)
5637 kw[iz] /= kmax;
5638}

◆ read_met()

int read_met ( const char *  filename,
ctl_t *  ctl,
clim_t *  clim,
met_t *  met 
)

Reads meteorological data from a file and populates the provided structures.

This function reads meteorological data from a specified file, populating the provided structures ctl, clim, and met with the parsed data. The function handles both netCDF and binary data formats. For netCDF data, it performs various data processing steps such as reading coordinates, surface data, atmospheric levels, and calculating derived quantities. For binary data, it reads the data directly into the appropriate arrays and structures.

Parameters
filenameA string containing the path to the file containing meteorological data.
ctlA pointer to a structure containing control parameters.
climA pointer to a structure containing climatological data.
metA pointer to a structure to store the meteorological data.
Returns
Returns 1 on success and 0 on failure.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Logs information indicating the meteorological data file being read.
  • Determines the type of meteorological data (netCDF or binary).
  • For netCDF data:
    • Opens the netCDF file and reads various data components including grid coordinates, atmospheric levels, surface data, and derived quantities.
    • Performs data processing steps such as extrapolation, boundary condition handling, downsampling, and calculations of geopotential heights, potential vorticity, boundary layer data, tropopause data, cloud properties, convective available potential energy, total column ozone, and detrending.
    • Checks and adjusts meteorological data as necessary, including monotonicity of vertical coordinates.
    • Closes the netCDF file after reading.
  • For binary data:
    • Opens the binary file and reads metadata including the type and version of the data, time information, dimensions, and grid coordinates.
    • Reads surface data, level data, and final flag from the binary file.
    • Closes the binary file after reading.
  • Copies wind data to a cache for subsequent use.
  • Returns 1 on successful reading and processing of meteorological data.
Note
The function handles different types of meteorological data formats and performs appropriate processing and error checking for each format.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 5642 of file mptrac.c.

5646 {
5647
5648 /* Write info... */
5649 LOG(1, "Read meteo data: %s", filename);
5650
5651 /* Initialize rank... */
5652 int rank = 0;
5653#ifdef MPI
5654 if (ctl->met_mpi_share)
5655 MPI_Comm_rank(MPI_COMM_WORLD, &rank);
5656#endif
5657
5658 /* Read netCDF data... */
5659 if (!ctl->met_mpi_share || rank == 0) {
5660 if (ctl->met_type == 0) {
5661
5662 int ncid;
5663
5664 /* Open netCDF file... */
5665 if (nc_open(filename, NC_NOWRITE, &ncid) != NC_NOERR) {
5666 WARN("Cannot open file!");
5667 return 0;
5668 }
5669
5670 /* Read coordinates of meteo data... */
5671 read_met_grid(filename, ncid, ctl, met);
5672
5673 /* Read meteo data on vertical levels... */
5674 read_met_levels(ncid, ctl, met);
5675
5676 /* Extrapolate data for lower boundary... */
5677 read_met_extrapolate(met);
5678
5679 /* Read surface data... */
5680 read_met_surface(ncid, ctl, met);
5681
5682 /* Fix polar winds... */
5683 read_met_polar_winds(met);
5684
5685 /* Create periodic boundary conditions... */
5686 read_met_periodic(met);
5687
5688 /* Downsampling... */
5689 read_met_sample(ctl, met);
5690
5691 /* Calculate geopotential heights... */
5692 read_met_geopot(ctl, met);
5693
5694 /* Calculate potential vorticity... */
5695 read_met_pv(met);
5696
5697 /* Calculate boundary layer data... */
5698 read_met_pbl(met);
5699
5700 /* Calculate tropopause data... */
5701 read_met_tropo(ctl, clim, met);
5702
5703 /* Calculate cloud properties... */
5704 read_met_cloud(met);
5705
5706 /* Calculate convective available potential energy... */
5707 read_met_cape(clim, met);
5708
5709 /* Calculate total column ozone... */
5710 read_met_ozone(met);
5711
5712 /* Detrending... */
5713 read_met_detrend(ctl, met);
5714
5715 /* Check meteo data and smooth zeta profiles ... */
5716 if (ctl->advect_vert_coord == 1)
5717 read_met_monotonize(met);
5718
5719 /* Close file... */
5720 NC(nc_close(ncid));
5721 }
5722
5723 /* Read binary data... */
5724 else if (ctl->met_type >= 1 && ctl->met_type <= 5) {
5725
5726 FILE *in;
5727
5728 double r;
5729
5730 int year, mon, day, hour, min, sec;
5731
5732 /* Set timer... */
5733 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_BIN", "INPUT", NVTX_READ);
5734
5735 /* Open file... */
5736 if (!(in = fopen(filename, "r"))) {
5737 WARN("Cannot open file!");
5738 return 0;
5739 }
5740
5741 /* Check type of binary data... */
5742 int met_type;
5743 FREAD(&met_type, int,
5744 1,
5745 in);
5746 if (met_type != ctl->met_type)
5747 ERRMSG("Wrong MET_TYPE of binary data!");
5748
5749 /* Check version of binary data... */
5750 int version;
5751 FREAD(&version, int,
5752 1,
5753 in);
5754 if (version != 100 && version != 101 && version != 102)
5755 ERRMSG("Wrong version of binary data!");
5756
5757 /* Read time... */
5758 FREAD(&met->time, double,
5759 1,
5760 in);
5761 jsec2time(met->time, &year, &mon, &day, &hour, &min, &sec, &r);
5762 LOG(2, "Time: %.2f (%d-%02d-%02d, %02d:%02d UTC)",
5763 met->time, year, mon, day, hour, min);
5764 if (year < 1900 || year > 2100 || mon < 1 || mon > 12
5765 || day < 1 || day > 31 || hour < 0 || hour > 23)
5766 ERRMSG("Error while reading time!");
5767
5768 /* Read dimensions... */
5769 FREAD(&met->nx, int,
5770 1,
5771 in);
5772 LOG(2, "Number of longitudes: %d", met->nx);
5773 if (met->nx < 2 || met->nx > EX)
5774 ERRMSG("Number of longitudes out of range!");
5775
5776 FREAD(&met->ny, int,
5777 1,
5778 in);
5779 LOG(2, "Number of latitudes: %d", met->ny);
5780 if (met->ny < 2 || met->ny > EY)
5781 ERRMSG("Number of latitudes out of range!");
5782
5783 FREAD(&met->np, int,
5784 1,
5785 in);
5786 LOG(2, "Number of levels: %d", met->np);
5787 if (met->np < 2 || met->np > EP)
5788 ERRMSG("Number of levels out of range!");
5789
5790 /* Read grid... */
5791 FREAD(met->lon, double,
5792 (size_t) met->nx,
5793 in);
5794 LOG(2, "Longitudes: %g, %g ... %g deg",
5795 met->lon[0], met->lon[1], met->lon[met->nx - 1]);
5796
5797 FREAD(met->lat, double,
5798 (size_t) met->ny,
5799 in);
5800 LOG(2, "Latitudes: %g, %g ... %g deg",
5801 met->lat[0], met->lat[1], met->lat[met->ny - 1]);
5802
5803 FREAD(met->p, double,
5804 (size_t) met->np,
5805 in);
5806 LOG(2, "Altitude levels: %g, %g ... %g km",
5807 Z(met->p[0]), Z(met->p[1]), Z(met->p[met->np - 1]));
5808 LOG(2, "Pressure levels: %g, %g ... %g hPa",
5809 met->p[0], met->p[1], met->p[met->np - 1]);
5810
5811 /* Read surface data... */
5812 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->ps, "PS");
5813 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->ts, "TS");
5814 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->zs, "ZS");
5815 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->us, "US");
5816 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->vs, "VS");
5817 if (version >= 101) {
5818 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->lsm, "LSM");
5819 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->sst, "SST");
5820 }
5821 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->pbl, "PBL");
5822 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->pt, "PT");
5823 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->tt, "TT");

5832 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->cape, "CAPE");
5833 read_met_bin_2d(in, met, met->cin, "CIN");
5834
5835 /* Read level data... */
5836 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->z, "Z", -1e34f, 1e34f);
5837 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->t, "T", 0, 1e34f);
5838 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->u, "U", -1e34f, 1e34f);

5840 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->w, "W", -1e34f, 1e34f);
5841 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->pv, "PV", -1e34f, 1e34f);
5842 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->h2o, "H2O", 0, 1e34f);
5843 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->o3, "O3", 0, 1e34f);
5844 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->lwc, "LWC", 0, 1e34f);
5845 if (version >= 102)
5846 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->rwc, "RWC", 0, 1e34f);
5847 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->iwc, "IWC", 0, 1e34f);
5848 if (version >= 102)

5850 if (version >= 101)
5851 read_met_bin_3d(in, ctl, met, met->cc, "CC", 0, 1);
5852
5853 /* Read final flag... */
5854 int final;
5855 FREAD(&final, int,
5856 1,
5857 in);
5858 if (final != 999)
5859 ERRMSG("Error while reading binary data!");
5860
5861 /* Close file... */
5862 fclose(in);
5863 }
5864
5865 /* Not implemented... */
5866 else
5867 ERRMSG("MET_TYPE not implemented!");
5868 }
5869
5870 /* Broadcast data via MPI... */
5871#ifdef MPI
5872 if (ctl->met_mpi_share) {
5873

◆ read_met_bin_2d()

void read_met_bin_2d ( FILE *  in,
const met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY],
const char *  varname 
)

Reads a 2-dimensional meteorological variable from a binary file and stores it in the provided array.

This function reads a 2-dimensional meteorological variable from a binary file, which is assumed to be uncompressed, and stores it in the provided 2-dimensional array var. The variable name is used for logging purposes to identify the data being read.

Parameters
inA pointer to the FILE structure representing the binary file to read from.
metA pointer to a structure containing meteorological data.
varA 2-dimensional array to store the read variable.
varnameA string containing the name of the variable being read.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Allocates memory for a temporary buffer to hold the uncompressed data.
  • Logs information about the variable being read from the file.
  • Reads the uncompressed data from the file into the temporary buffer.
  • Copies the data from the temporary buffer to the provided 2-dimensional array.
  • Frees the memory allocated for the temporary buffer.
Note
The function assumes that the binary file contains uncompressed data and reads the data directly into the provided array without any additional processing.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 5889 of file mptrac.c.

5894
5895 float *help;
5896
5897 /* Allocate... */
5898 ALLOC(help, float,
5899 EX * EY);
5900
5901 /* Read uncompressed... */
5902 LOG(2, "Read 2-D variable: %s (uncompressed)", varname);
5903 FREAD(help, float,
5904 (size_t) (met->nx * met->ny),
5905 in);
5906
5907 /* Copy data... */
5908 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
5909 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
5910 var[ix][iy] = help[ARRAY_2D(ix, iy, met->ny)];
5911
5912 /* Free... */
5913 free(help);
5914}

◆ read_met_bin_3d()

void read_met_bin_3d ( FILE *  in,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY][EP],
const char *  varname,
const float  bound_min,
const float  bound_max 
)

Reads 3D meteorological data from a binary file, potentially using different compression methods.

This function reads 3-dimensional meteorological data from a binary file into a specified variable array. The data can be read in uncompressed form or using one of several supported compression methods. The data is then clamped to specified minimum and maximum bounds.

Parameters
[in]inPointer to the input file from which to read the data.
[in]ctlPointer to the control structure that contains metadata about the type of data and how it is stored.
[in]metPointer to the meteorological structure that contains the dimensions of the data.
[out]var3D array to store the read data, with dimensions [EX][EY][EP].
[in]varnameName of the variable being read, used for logging and debugging.
[in]bound_minMinimum bound to which data values should be clamped.
[in]bound_maxMaximum bound to which data values should be clamped.

The function supports the following types of data:

  • Uncompressed data
  • Packed data
  • ZFP compressed data (if compiled with ZFP support)
  • ZSTD compressed data (if compiled with ZSTD support)
  • CMULTISCALE compressed data (if compiled with CMS support)

Depending on the compression type specified in the control structure, the appropriate reading and decompression function is used. The data is read into a temporary buffer, then copied into the output array, applying the specified bounds to each value.

Note
The function assumes that the dimensions EX, EY, and EP are correctly defined and match the dimensions specified in the met structure.
If the appropriate compression support is not compiled, an error message is generated.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 5918 of file mptrac.c.

5925 {
5926
5927 float *help;
5928
5929 /* Allocate... */
5930 ALLOC(help, float,
5931 EX * EY * EP);
5932
5933 /* Read uncompressed data... */
5934 if (ctl->met_type == 1) {
5935 LOG(2, "Read 3-D variable: %s (uncompressed)", varname);
5936 FREAD(help, float,
5937 (size_t) (met->nx * met->ny * met->np),
5938 in);
5939 }
5940
5941 /* Read packed data... */
5942 else if (ctl->met_type == 2)
5943 compress_pck(varname, help, (size_t) (met->ny * met->nx),
5944 (size_t) met->np, 1, in);
5945
5946 /* Read zfp data... */
5947 else if (ctl->met_type == 3) {
5948#ifdef ZFP
5949 int precision;
5950 FREAD(&precision, int,
5951 1,
5952 in);
5953
5954 double tolerance;
5955 FREAD(&tolerance, double,
5956 1,
5957 in);
5958
5959 compress_zfp(varname, help, met->np, met->ny, met->nx, precision,
5960 tolerance, 1, in);
5961#else
5962 ERRMSG("MPTRAC was compiled without zfp compression!");
5963#endif
5964 }
5965
5966 /* Read zstd data... */
5967 else if (ctl->met_type == 4) {
5968#ifdef ZSTD
5969 compress_zstd(varname, help, (size_t) (met->np * met->ny * met->nx), 1,
5970 in);
5971#else
5972 ERRMSG("MPTRAC was compiled without zstd compression!");
5973#endif
5974 }
5975
5976 /* Read cmultiscale data... */
5977 else if (ctl->met_type == 5) {
5978#ifdef CMS
5979 compress_cms(ctl, varname, help, (size_t) met->nx, (size_t) met->ny,
5980 (size_t) met->np, 1, in);
5981#else
5982 ERRMSG("MPTRAC was compiled without cmultiscale compression!");
5983#endif
5984 }
5985
5986 /* Copy data... */
5987#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
5988 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
5989 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
5990 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
5991 var[ix][iy][ip] = help[ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, met->ny, ip, met->np)];
5992 if (var[ix][iy][ip] < bound_min)
5993 var[ix][iy][ip] = bound_min;
5994 else if (var[ix][iy][ip] > bound_max)
5995 var[ix][iy][ip] = bound_max;
5996 }
5997
5998 /* Free... */
5999 free(help);
6000}

◆ read_met_cape()

void read_met_cape ( const clim_t *  clim,
met_t *  met 
)

Calculates Convective Available Potential Energy (CAPE) for each grid point.

This function calculates the Convective Available Potential Energy (CAPE) at each grid point based on the provided meteorological data. CAPE is a measure of the energy available for deep convection, which is essential for severe weather development.

Parameters
climA pointer to a structure containing climatological data.
metA pointer to a structure containing meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets up a timer to monitor the calculation time.
  • Initializes variables and constants required for the computation, such as vertical spacing and pressure levels.
  • Iterates over each grid point in parallel using OpenMP.
  • Calculates CAPE by integrating the difference in virtual temperatures between the environment and the parcel, up to the level of free convection (LFC).
  • Determines the lifted condensation level (LCL), level of free convection (LFC), equilibrium level (EL), and Convective Inhibition (CIN) for each grid point.
  • Checks the results and updates the corresponding fields in the meteorological data structure.
Note
The function utilizes OpenMP for parallelization to enhance performance by distributing the computation across multiple threads.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 6004 of file mptrac.c.

6006 {
6007
6008 /* Set timer... */
6009 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_CAPE", "METPROC", NVTX_READ);
6010 LOG(2, "Calculate CAPE...");
6011
6012 /* Vertical spacing (about 100 m)... */
6013 const double pfac = 1.01439, dz0 = RI / MA / G0 * log(pfac);
6014
6015 /* Loop over columns... */
6016#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6017 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6018 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6019
6020 /* Get potential temperature and water vapor at lowest 50 hPa... */
6021 int n = 0;
6022 double h2o = 0, t, theta = 0;
6023 double pbot = MIN(met->ps[ix][iy], met->p[0]);
6024 double ptop = pbot - 50.;
6025 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6026 if (met->p[ip] <= pbot) {
6027 theta += THETA(met->p[ip], met->t[ix][iy][ip]);
6028 h2o += met->h2o[ix][iy][ip];
6029 n++;
6030 }
6031 if (met->p[ip] < ptop && n > 0)
6032 break;
6033 }
6034 theta /= n;
6035 h2o /= n;
6036
6037 /* Cannot compute anything if water vapor is missing... */
6038 met->plcl[ix][iy] = NAN;
6039 met->plfc[ix][iy] = NAN;
6040 met->pel[ix][iy] = NAN;
6041 met->cape[ix][iy] = NAN;
6042 met->cin[ix][iy] = NAN;
6043 if (h2o <= 0)
6044 continue;
6045
6046 /* Find lifted condensation level (LCL)... */
6047 ptop = P(20.);
6048 pbot = met->ps[ix][iy];
6049 do {
6050 met->plcl[ix][iy] = (float) (0.5 * (pbot + ptop));
6051 t = theta / pow(1000. / met->plcl[ix][iy], 0.286);
6052 if (RH(met->plcl[ix][iy], t, h2o) > 100.)
6053 ptop = met->plcl[ix][iy];
6054 else
6055 pbot = met->plcl[ix][iy];
6056 } while (pbot - ptop > 0.1);
6057
6058 /* Calculate CIN up to LCL... */
6059 INTPOL_INIT;
6060 double dcape, dz, h2o_env, t_env;
6061 double p = met->ps[ix][iy];
6062 met->cape[ix][iy] = met->cin[ix][iy] = 0;
6063 do {
6064 dz = dz0 * TVIRT(t, h2o);
6065 p /= pfac;
6066 t = theta / pow(1000. / p, 0.286);
6067 intpol_met_space_3d(met, met->t, p, met->lon[ix], met->lat[iy],
6068 &t_env, ci, cw, 1);
6069 intpol_met_space_3d(met, met->h2o, p, met->lon[ix], met->lat[iy],
6070 &h2o_env, ci, cw, 0);
6071 dcape = 1e3 * G0 * (TVIRT(t, h2o) - TVIRT(t_env, h2o_env)) /
6072 TVIRT(t_env, h2o_env) * dz;
6073 if (dcape < 0)
6074 met->cin[ix][iy] += fabsf((float) dcape);
6075 } while (p > met->plcl[ix][iy]);
6076
6077 /* Calculate level of free convection (LFC), equilibrium level (EL),
6078 and convective available potential energy (CAPE)... */
6079 dcape = 0;
6080 p = met->plcl[ix][iy];
6081 t = theta / pow(1000. / p, 0.286);
6082 ptop = 0.75 * clim_tropo(clim, met->time, met->lat[iy]);
6083 do {
6084 dz = dz0 * TVIRT(t, h2o);
6085 p /= pfac;
6086 t -= lapse_rate(t, h2o) * dz;
6087 double psat = PSAT(t);
6088 h2o = psat / (p - (1. - EPS) * psat);
6089 intpol_met_space_3d(met, met->t, p, met->lon[ix], met->lat[iy],
6090 &t_env, ci, cw, 1);
6091 intpol_met_space_3d(met, met->h2o, p, met->lon[ix], met->lat[iy],
6092 &h2o_env, ci, cw, 0);
6093 double dcape_old = dcape;
6094 dcape = 1e3 * G0 * (TVIRT(t, h2o) - TVIRT(t_env, h2o_env)) /
6095 TVIRT(t_env, h2o_env) * dz;
6096 if (dcape > 0) {
6097 met->cape[ix][iy] += (float) dcape;
6098 if (!isfinite(met->plfc[ix][iy]))
6099 met->plfc[ix][iy] = (float) p;
6100 } else if (dcape_old > 0)
6101 met->pel[ix][iy] = (float) p;
6102 if (dcape < 0 && !isfinite(met->plfc[ix][iy]))
6103 met->cin[ix][iy] += fabsf((float) dcape);
6104 } while (p > ptop);
6105
6106 /* Check results... */
6107 if (!isfinite(met->plfc[ix][iy]))
6108 met->cin[ix][iy] = NAN;
6109 }
6110}

double clim_tropo(const clim_t *clim, const double t, const double lat)

◆ read_met_cloud()

void read_met_cloud ( met_t *  met)

Calculates cloud-related variables for each grid point.

This function calculates cloud-related variables, such as cloud cover, cloud top pressure, cloud bottom pressure, and total cloud water content, based on the provided meteorological data.

Parameters
metA pointer to a structure containing meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets up a timer to monitor the calculation time.
  • Initializes variables and constants required for the computation.
  • Iterates over each grid point in parallel using OpenMP.
  • Determines cloud-related variables based on thresholds for liquid water content (LWC), rain water content (RWC), ice water content (IWC) and snow water content (SWC).
  • Calculates cloud cover, cloud top pressure, cloud bottom pressure, and total cloud water content for each grid point.
  • Updates the corresponding fields in the meteorological data structure.
Note
The function utilizes OpenMP for parallelization to enhance performance by distributing the computation across multiple threads.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 6114 of file mptrac.c.

6115 {
6116
6117 /* Set timer... */
6118 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_CLOUD", "METPROC", NVTX_READ);
6119 LOG(2, "Calculate cloud data...");
6120
6121 /* Loop over columns... */
6122#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6123 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6124 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6125
6126 /* Init... */
6127 met->pct[ix][iy] = NAN;
6128 met->pcb[ix][iy] = NAN;
6129 met->cl[ix][iy] = 0;
6130
6131 /* Loop over pressure levels... */
6132 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np - 1; ip++) {
6133
6134 /* Check pressure... */
6135 if (met->p[ip] > met->ps[ix][iy] || met->p[ip] < P(20.))
6136 continue;
6137
6138 /* Check ice water and liquid water content... */
6139 if (met->iwc[ix][iy][ip] > 0 || met->rwc[ix][iy][ip] > 0
6140 || met->lwc[ix][iy][ip] > 0 || met->swc[ix][iy][ip] > 0) {
6141
6142 /* Get cloud top pressure ... */
6143 met->pct[ix][iy]
6144 = (float) (0.5 * (met->p[ip] + (float) met->p[ip + 1]));
6145
6146 /* Get cloud bottom pressure ... */
6147 if (!isfinite(met->pcb[ix][iy]))
6148 met->pcb[ix][iy]
6149 = (float) (0.5 * (met->p[ip] + met->p[MAX(ip - 1, 0)]));
6150 }
6151
6152 /* Get cloud water... */
6153 met->cl[ix][iy] += (float)
6154 (0.5 * (met->lwc[ix][iy][ip] + met->lwc[ix][iy][ip + 1]
6155 + met->rwc[ix][iy][ip] + met->rwc[ix][iy][ip + 1]
6156 + met->iwc[ix][iy][ip] + met->iwc[ix][iy][ip + 1]
6157 + met->swc[ix][iy][ip] + met->swc[ix][iy][ip + 1])
6158 * 100. * (met->p[ip] - met->p[ip + 1]) / G0);
6159 }
6160 }
6161}

◆ read_met_detrend()

void read_met_detrend ( const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met 
)

Detrends meteorological data.

This function detrends meteorological data by removing spatially varying backgrounds from each grid point. Detrending helps in removing systematic biases and trends from the data, enabling better analysis and modeling.

Parameters
ctlA pointer to a structure containing control parameters.
metA pointer to a structure containing meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Checks if detrending is enabled based on the control parameters.
  • Sets up a timer to monitor the detrending time.
  • Allocates memory for a temporary meteorological data structure.
  • Calculates the standard deviation and box size for detrending.
  • Calculates the detrended data by subtracting spatially varying backgrounds.
  • Updates the original meteorological data with the detrended values.
  • Frees the allocated memory.
Note
Detrending is performed by subtracting spatially varying backgrounds calculated from neighboring grid points.
OpenMP is utilized for parallelization to enhance performance by distributing the computation across multiple threads.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 6165 of file mptrac.c.

6167 {
6168
6169 met_t *help;
6170
6171 /* Check parameters... */
6172 if (ctl->met_detrend <= 0)
6173 return;
6174
6175 /* Set timer... */
6176 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_DETREND", "METPROC", NVTX_READ);
6177 LOG(2, "Detrend meteo data...");
6178
6179 /* Allocate... */
6180 ALLOC(help, met_t, 1);
6181
6182 /* Calculate standard deviation... */
6183 const double sigma = ctl->met_detrend / 2.355;
6184 const double tssq = 2. * SQR(sigma);
6185
6186 /* Calculate box size in latitude... */
6187 int sy = (int) (3. * DY2DEG(sigma) / fabs(met->lat[1] - met->lat[0]));
6188 sy = MIN(MAX(1, sy), met->ny / 2);
6189
6190 /* Calculate background... */
6191#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6192 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++) {
6193 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6194
6195 /* Calculate Cartesian coordinates... */
6196 double x0[3];
6197 geo2cart(0.0, met->lon[ix], met->lat[iy], x0);
6198
6199 /* Calculate box size in longitude... */
6200 int sx =
6201 (int) (3. * DX2DEG(sigma, met->lat[iy]) /
6202 fabs(met->lon[1] - met->lon[0]));
6203 sx = MIN(MAX(1, sx), met->nx / 2);
6204
6205 /* Init... */
6206 float wsum = 0;
6207 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6208 help->t[ix][iy][ip] = 0;
6209 help->u[ix][iy][ip] = 0;
6210 help->v[ix][iy][ip] = 0;
6211 help->w[ix][iy][ip] = 0;
6212 }
6213
6214 /* Loop over neighboring grid points... */
6215 for (int ix2 = ix - sx; ix2 <= ix + sx; ix2++) {
6216 int ix3 = ix2;
6217 if (ix3 < 0)
6218 ix3 += met->nx;
6219 else if (ix3 >= met->nx)
6220 ix3 -= met->nx;
6221 for (int iy2 = MAX(iy - sy, 0);
6222 iy2 <= MIN(iy + sy, met->ny - 1); iy2++) {
6223
6224 /* Calculate Cartesian coordinates... */
6225 double x1[3];
6226 geo2cart(0.0, met->lon[ix3], met->lat[iy2], x1);
6227
6228 /* Calculate weighting factor... */
6229 const float w = (float) exp(-DIST2(x0, x1) / tssq);
6230
6231 /* Add data... */
6232 wsum += w;
6233 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6234 help->t[ix][iy][ip] += w * met->t[ix3][iy2][ip];
6235 help->u[ix][iy][ip] += w * met->u[ix3][iy2][ip];
6236 help->v[ix][iy][ip] += w * met->v[ix3][iy2][ip];
6237 help->w[ix][iy][ip] += w * met->w[ix3][iy2][ip];
6238 }
6239 }
6240 }
6241
6242 /* Normalize... */
6243 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6244 help->t[ix][iy][ip] /= wsum;
6245 help->u[ix][iy][ip] /= wsum;
6246 help->v[ix][iy][ip] /= wsum;
6247 help->w[ix][iy][ip] /= wsum;
6248 }
6249 }
6250 }
6251
6252 /* Subtract background... */
6253#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(3)
6254 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6255 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
6256 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6257 met->t[ix][iy][ip] -= help->t[ix][iy][ip];
6258 met->u[ix][iy][ip] -= help->u[ix][iy][ip];
6259 met->v[ix][iy][ip] -= help->v[ix][iy][ip];
6260 met->w[ix][iy][ip] -= help->w[ix][iy][ip];
6261 }
6262
6263 /* Free... */
6264 free(help);
6265}

◆ read_met_extrapolate()

void read_met_extrapolate ( met_t *  met)

Extrapolates meteorological data.

This function extrapolates meteorological data by filling missing or invalid data points with values from the nearest valid point above. Extrapolation is performed column-wise, ensuring that missing data points are replaced with valid values.

Parameters
metA pointer to a structure containing meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets up a timer to monitor the extrapolation time.
  • Loops over each grid column in parallel.
  • Finds the lowest valid data point within each column.
  • Extrapolates missing or invalid data points by copying values from the nearest valid point above.
  • Updates the meteorological data structure with the extrapolated values.
Note
Extrapolation is performed by copying values from the nearest valid point above to fill missing or invalid data points. OpenMP is utilized for parallelization to enhance performance by distributing the computation across multiple threads.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 6269 of file mptrac.c.

6270 {
6271
6272 /* Set timer... */
6273 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_EXTRAPOLATE", "METPROC", NVTX_READ);
6274 LOG(2, "Extrapolate meteo data...");
6275
6276 /* Loop over columns... */
6277#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6278 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6279 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6280
6281 /* Find lowest valid data point... */
6282 int ip0;
6283 for (ip0 = met->np - 1; ip0 >= 0; ip0--)
6284 if (!isfinite(met->t[ix][iy][ip0])
6285 || !isfinite(met->u[ix][iy][ip0])
6286 || !isfinite(met->v[ix][iy][ip0])
6287 || !isfinite(met->w[ix][iy][ip0]))
6288 break;
6289
6290 /* Extrapolate... */
6291 for (int ip = ip0; ip >= 0; ip--) {
6292 met->t[ix][iy][ip] = met->t[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6293 met->u[ix][iy][ip] = met->u[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6294 met->v[ix][iy][ip] = met->v[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6295 met->w[ix][iy][ip] = met->w[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6296 met->h2o[ix][iy][ip] = met->h2o[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6297 met->o3[ix][iy][ip] = met->o3[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6298 met->lwc[ix][iy][ip] = met->lwc[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6299 met->rwc[ix][iy][ip] = met->rwc[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6300 met->iwc[ix][iy][ip] = met->iwc[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6301 met->swc[ix][iy][ip] = met->swc[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6302 met->cc[ix][iy][ip] = met->cc[ix][iy][ip + 1];
6303 }
6304 }
6305}

◆ read_met_geopot()

void read_met_geopot ( const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met 
)

Calculates geopotential heights from meteorological data.

This function calculates geopotential heights from provided meteorological data using the hydrostatic equation. Geopotential heights are computed column-wise for each grid point based on the temperature, pressure, and surface height information. Optionally, the calculated geopotential heights can be smoothed horizontally using a weighted averaging scheme.

Parameters
ctlA pointer to a structure containing control parameters.
metA pointer to a structure containing meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets up a timer to monitor the geopotential height calculation time.
  • Calculates the logarithm of pressure levels for efficient computation.
  • Applies the hydrostatic equation to determine geopotential heights based on temperature, pressure, and height information.
  • Optionally, performs horizontal smoothing on the calculated geopotential heights.
  • Updates the meteorological data structure with the computed geopotential heights.
Note
The hydrostatic equation is utilized to calculate geopotential heights, ensuring consistency with atmospheric conditions. Optionally, horizontal smoothing can be applied to the calculated geopotential heights to reduce spatial variability. OpenMP is utilized for parallelization to enhance performance by distributing the computation across multiple threads.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 6309 of file mptrac.c.

6311 {
6312
6313 float *help;
6314
6315 double logp[EP];
6316
6317 int dx = ctl->met_geopot_sx, dy = ctl->met_geopot_sy;
6318
6319 /* Set timer... */
6320 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_GEOPOT", "METPROC", NVTX_READ);
6321 LOG(2, "Calculate geopotential heights...");
6322
6323 /* Allocate... */
6324 ALLOC(help, float,
6325 EX * EY * EP);
6326
6327 /* Calculate log pressure... */
6328#pragma omp parallel for default(shared)
6329 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
6330 logp[ip] = log(met->p[ip]);
6331
6332 /* Apply hydrostatic equation to calculate geopotential heights... */
6333#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6334 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6335 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6336
6337 /* Get surface height and pressure... */
6338 const double zs = met->zs[ix][iy];
6339 const double lnps = log(met->ps[ix][iy]);
6340
6341 /* Get temperature and water vapor at the surface... */
6342 const int ip0 = locate_irr(met->p, met->np, met->ps[ix][iy]);
6343 const double ts = LIN(met->p[ip0], met->t[ix][iy][ip0], met->p[ip0 + 1],
6344 met->t[ix][iy][ip0 + 1], met->ps[ix][iy]);
6345 const double h2os =
6346 LIN(met->p[ip0], met->h2o[ix][iy][ip0], met->p[ip0 + 1],
6347 met->h2o[ix][iy][ip0 + 1], met->ps[ix][iy]);
6348
6349 /* Upper part of profile... */
6350 met->z[ix][iy][ip0 + 1]
6351 = (float) (zs +
6352 ZDIFF(lnps, ts, h2os, logp[ip0 + 1],
6353 met->t[ix][iy][ip0 + 1], met->h2o[ix][iy][ip0 + 1]));
6354 for (int ip = ip0 + 2; ip < met->np; ip++)
6355 met->z[ix][iy][ip]
6356 = (float) (met->z[ix][iy][ip - 1] +
6357 ZDIFF(logp[ip - 1], met->t[ix][iy][ip - 1],
6358 met->h2o[ix][iy][ip - 1], logp[ip],
6359 met->t[ix][iy][ip], met->h2o[ix][iy][ip]));
6360
6361 /* Lower part of profile... */
6362 met->z[ix][iy][ip0]
6363 = (float) (zs +
6364 ZDIFF(lnps, ts, h2os, logp[ip0],
6365 met->t[ix][iy][ip0], met->h2o[ix][iy][ip0]));
6366 for (int ip = ip0 - 1; ip >= 0; ip--)
6367 met->z[ix][iy][ip]
6368 = (float) (met->z[ix][iy][ip + 1] +
6369 ZDIFF(logp[ip + 1], met->t[ix][iy][ip + 1],
6370 met->h2o[ix][iy][ip + 1], logp[ip],
6371 met->t[ix][iy][ip], met->h2o[ix][iy][ip]));
6372 }
6373
6374 /* Check control parameters... */
6375 if (dx == 0 || dy == 0)
6376 return;
6377
6378 /* Default smoothing parameters... */
6379 if (dx < 0 || dy < 0) {
6380 if (fabs(met->lon[1] - met->lon[0]) < 0.5) {
6381 dx = 3;
6382 dy = 2;
6383 } else {
6384 dx = 6;
6385 dy = 4;
6386 }
6387 }
6388
6389 /* Calculate weights for smoothing... */
6390 float ws[dx + 1][dy + 1];
6391#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6392 for (int ix = 0; ix <= dx; ix++)
6393 for (int iy = 0; iy < dy; iy++)
6394 ws[ix][iy] = (1.0f - (float) ix / (float) dx)
6395 * (1.0f - (float) iy / (float) dy);
6396
6397 /* Copy data... */
6398#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(3)
6399 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6400 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
6401 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
6402 help[ARRAY_3D(ip, ix, met->nx, iy, met->ny)] = met->z[ix][iy][ip];
6403
6404 /* Horizontal smoothing... */
6405#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(3)
6406 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
6407 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6408 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6409 float res = 0, wsum = 0;
6410 int iy0 = MAX(iy - dy + 1, 0);
6411 int iy1 = MIN(iy + dy - 1, met->ny - 1);
6412 for (int ix2 = ix - dx + 1; ix2 <= ix + dx - 1; ++ix2) {
6413 int ix3 = ix2;
6414 if (ix3 < 0)
6415 ix3 += met->nx;
6416 else if (ix3 >= met->nx)
6417 ix3 -= met->nx;
6418 for (int iy2 = iy0; iy2 <= iy1; ++iy2)
6419 if (isfinite(help[ARRAY_3D(ip, ix3, met->nx, iy2, met->ny)])) {
6420 float w = ws[abs(ix - ix2)][abs(iy - iy2)];
6421 res += w * help[ARRAY_3D(ip, ix3, met->nx, iy2, met->ny)];
6422 wsum += w;
6423 }
6424 }
6425 if (wsum > 0)
6426 met->z[ix][iy][ip] = res / wsum;
6427 else
6428 met->z[ix][iy][ip] = NAN;
6429 }
6430
6431 /* Free... */
6432 free(help);
6433}

#define ZDIFF(lnp0, t0, h2o0, lnp1, t1, h2o1)

◆ read_met_grid()

void read_met_grid ( const char *  filename,
const int  ncid,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met 
)

Reads meteorological grid information from a NetCDF file.

This function reads meteorological grid information from a NetCDF file, including time, spatial dimensions, and pressure levels. It also extracts longitudes, latitudes, and pressure levels from the NetCDF file based on the specified control parameters. The function determines the time information either from the filename or from the data file, depending on the file type.

Parameters
filenameThe filename of the NetCDF file.
ncidThe NetCDF file identifier.
ctlA pointer to a structure containing control parameters.
metA pointer to a structure to store meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets up a timer to monitor the reading time for meteorological grid information.
  • Determines the time information from either the filename or the data file based on the file type.
  • Checks the validity of the time information.
  • Retrieves grid dimensions (longitude, latitude, and vertical levels) from the NetCDF file.
  • Reads longitudes, latitudes, and pressure levels from the NetCDF file.
  • Converts pressure levels to hPa if necessary.
  • Logs the retrieved grid information for verification and debugging purposes.
Note
This function supports reading meteorological grid information from different types of NetCDF files, including MPTRAC and CLaMS. The time information is extracted either from the filename or from the data file, depending on the file type and control parameters. Spatial dimensions (longitude, latitude, and vertical levels) and pressure levels are retrieved from the NetCDF file.
Authors
Lars Hoffmann
Jan Clemens

Definition at line 6437 of file mptrac.c.

6492

6495 || day < 1 || day > 31 || hour < 0 || hour > 23)

6497 jsec2time(met->time, &year2, &mon2, &day2, &hour2, &min2, &sec2, &r2);

6504

6506 LOG(2, "Number of latitudes: %d", met->ny);

6509 sprintf(levname, "lev");
6510 if (nc_inq_dimid(ncid, levname, &dimid2) != NC_NOERR)
6511 sprintf(levname, "plev");
6512 if (nc_inq_dimid(ncid, levname, &dimid2) != NC_NOERR)
6513 sprintf(levname, "hybrid");
6514
6515 NC_INQ_DIM(levname, &met->np, 1, EP);
6516 if (met->np == 1) {
6517 sprintf(levname, "lev_2");
6518 if (nc_inq_dimid(ncid, levname, &dimid2) != NC_NOERR) {
6519 sprintf(levname, "plev");
6520 NC(nc_inq_dimid(ncid, levname, &dimid2));
6521 }
6522 NC(nc_inq_dimlen(ncid, dimid2, &np));
6523 met->np = (int) np;
6524 }
6525 LOG(2, "Number of levels: %d", met->np);
6526 if (met->np < 2 || met->np > EP)
6527 ERRMSG("Number of levels out of range!");
6528
6529 /* Read longitudes and latitudes... */
6530 NC_GET_DOUBLE("lon", met->lon, 1);
6531 LOG(2, "Longitudes: %g, %g ... %g deg",
6532 met->lon[0], met->lon[1], met->lon[met->nx - 1]);
6533 NC_GET_DOUBLE("lat", met->lat, 1);
6534 LOG(2, "Latitudes: %g, %g ... %g deg",
6535 met->lat[0], met->lat[1], met->lat[met->ny - 1]);
6536
6537 /* Read pressure levels... */

6539 NC_GET_DOUBLE(levname, met->p, 1);
6540 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
6541 met->p[ip] /= 100.;
6542 LOG(2, "Altitude levels: %g, %g ... %g km",
6543 Z(met->p[0]), Z(met->p[1]), Z(met->p[met->np - 1]));
6544 LOG(2, "Pressure levels: %g, %g ... %g hPa",
6545 met->p[0], met->p[1], met->p[met->np - 1]);
6546 }
6547
6548 /* Read hybrid levels... */
6549 if (strcasecmp(levname, "hybrid") == 0)
6550 NC_GET_DOUBLE("hybrid", met->hybrid, 1);
6551}

◆ read_met_levels()

void read_met_levels ( const int  ncid,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met 
)

Reads meteorological variables at different vertical levels from a NetCDF file.

This function reads meteorological variables such as temperature, wind components, specific humidity, ozone data, cloud parameters, and cloud cover at various vertical levels from a NetCDF file. The function supports reading meteorological data from both MPTRAC and CLaMS formats. Depending on the file format, it reads specific variables and performs necessary conversions or interpolations.

Parameters
ncidThe NetCDF file identifier.
ctlA pointer to a structure containing control parameters.
metA pointer to a structure to store meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets up a timer to monitor the reading time for meteorological level data.
  • Reads meteorological variables from the NetCDF file based on the specified control parameters and file format.
  • Handles specific variables differently depending on the file format, such as reading temperature, wind components, humidity, ozone data, and cloud parameters.
  • Performs conversions or interpolations if necessary, such as converting specific humidity and ozone data from mixing ratio to volume mixing ratio.
  • Transfers velocity components to model levels for diabatic advection if applicable.
  • Reads pressure on model levels if specified in the control parameters.
  • Performs vertical interpolation from model levels to pressure levels if needed.
  • Checks the ordering of pressure levels to ensure they are in descending order.
Note
This function supports reading meteorological variables from NetCDF files in MPTRAC or CLaMS formats and handles specific variables differently based on the file format and control parameters. It performs necessary conversions or interpolations and ensures the correctness of pressure levels.
Authors
Lars Hoffmann
Jan Clemens

Definition at line 6555 of file mptrac.c.

6558 {
6559
6560 /* Set timer... */
6561 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_LEVELS", "INPUT", NVTX_READ);
6562 LOG(2, "Read level data...");
6563
6564 /* Read temperature... */
6565 if (!read_met_nc_3d(ncid, "t", "T", "temp", "TEMP", ctl, met, met->t, 1.0))
6566 ERRMSG("Cannot read temperature!");
6567
6568 /* Read horizontal wind and vertical velocity... */
6569 if (!read_met_nc_3d(ncid, "u", "U", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->u, 1.0))
6570 ERRMSG("Cannot read zonal wind!");
6571 if (!read_met_nc_3d(ncid, "v", "V", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->v, 1.0))
6572 ERRMSG("Cannot read meridional wind!");
6573 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6574 (ncid, "w", "W", "omega", "OMEGA", ctl, met, met->w, 0.01f))
6575 WARN("Cannot read vertical velocity!");
6576
6577 /* Read water vapor... */
6578 if (!ctl->met_relhum) {
6579 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6580 (ncid, "q", "Q", "sh", "SH", ctl, met, met->h2o, (float) (MA / MH2O)))
6581 WARN("Cannot read specific humidity!");
6582 } else {
6583 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6584 (ncid, "rh", "RH", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->h2o, 0.01f))
6585 WARN("Cannot read relative humidity!");
6586#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
6587 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6588 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
6589 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6590 double pw = met->h2o[ix][iy][ip] * PSAT(met->t[ix][iy][ip]);
6591 met->h2o[ix][iy][ip] =
6592 (float) (pw / (met->p[ip] - (1.0 - EPS) * pw));
6593 }
6594 }
6595
6596 /* Read ozone... */
6597 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6598 (ncid, "o3", "O3", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->o3, (float) (MA / MO3)))
6599 WARN("Cannot read ozone data!");
6600
6601 /* Read cloud data... */
6602 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6603 (ncid, "clwc", "CLWC", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->lwc, 1.0))
6604 WARN("Cannot read cloud liquid water content!");
6605 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6606 (ncid, "crwc", "CRWC", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->rwc, 1.0))
6607 WARN("Cannot read cloud rain water content!");
6608 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6609 (ncid, "ciwc", "CIWC", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->iwc, 1.0))
6610 WARN("Cannot read cloud ice water content!");
6611 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6612 (ncid, "cswc", "CSWC", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->swc, 1.0))
6613 WARN("Cannot read cloud snow water content!");
6614 if (!read_met_nc_3d(ncid, "cc", "CC", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->cc, 1.0))
6615 WARN("Cannot read cloud cover!");
6616
6617 /* Read zeta and zeta_dot... */
6618 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6619 (ncid, "ZETA", "zeta", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->zetal, 1.0))
6620 WARN("Cannot read ZETA!");
6621 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6622 (ncid, "ZETA_DOT_TOT", "ZETA_DOT_clr", "zeta_dot_clr",
6623 NULL, ctl, met, met->zeta_dotl, 0.00001157407f))
6624 WARN("Cannot read ZETA_DOT!");
6625
6626 /* Store velocities on model levels for diabatic advection... */
6627 if (ctl->met_vert_coord == 1) {
6628 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6629 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
6630 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++) {
6631 met->ul[ix][iy][ip] = met->u[ix][iy][ip];
6632 met->vl[ix][iy][ip] = met->v[ix][iy][ip];
6633 met->wl[ix][iy][ip] = met->w[ix][iy][ip];
6634 }
6635
6636 /* Original number of vertical levels... */
6637 met->npl = met->np;
6638 }
6639
6640 /* Read pressure on model levels... */
6641 if (ctl->met_np > 0 || ctl->met_vert_coord == 1) {
6642
6643 /* Read data... */
6644 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6645 (ncid, "pl", "PL", "pressure", "PRESSURE", ctl, met, met->pl, 0.01f))
6646 if (!read_met_nc_3d
6647 (ncid, "press", "PRESS", NULL, NULL, ctl, met, met->pl, 1.0))
6648 ERRMSG("Cannot read pressure on model levels!");
6649
6650 /* Check ordering of pressure levels... */
6651 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6652 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
6653 for (int ip = 1; ip < met->np; ip++)
6654 if ((met->pl[ix][iy][0] > met->pl[ix][iy][1]
6655 && met->pl[ix][iy][ip - 1] <= met->pl[ix][iy][ip])
6656 || (met->pl[ix][iy][0] < met->pl[ix][iy][1]
6657 && met->pl[ix][iy][ip - 1] >= met->pl[ix][iy][ip]))
6658 ERRMSG("Pressure profiles are not monotonic!");
6659 }
6660
6661 /* Interpolate from model levels to pressure levels... */
6662 if (ctl->met_np > 0) {
6663
6664 /* Interpolate variables... */
6665 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->t, "T");
6666 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->u, "U");
6667 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->v, "V");
6668 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->w, "W");
6669 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->h2o, "H2O");
6670 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->o3, "O3");
6671 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->lwc, "LWC");
6672 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->rwc, "RWC");
6673 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->iwc, "IWC");
6674 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->swc, "SWC");
6675 read_met_ml2pl(ctl, met, met->cc, "CC");
6676
6677 /* Set new pressure levels... */
6678 met->np = ctl->met_np;
6679 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
6680 met->p[ip] = ctl->met_p[ip];
6681 }
6682
6683 /* Check ordering of pressure levels... */
6684 for (int ip = 1; ip < met->np; ip++)
6685 if (met->p[ip - 1] < met->p[ip])
6686 ERRMSG("Pressure levels must be descending!");
6687}

◆ read_met_ml2pl()

void read_met_ml2pl ( const ctl_t *  ctl,
const met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY][EP],
const char *  varname 
)

Interpolates meteorological data to specified pressure levels.

This function interpolates meteorological data from model levels to pressure levels. The interpolation is performed in parallel over the spatial grid defined in the meteorological data structure.

Parameters
[in]ctlA pointer to a control structure containing the number of pressure levels (met_np) and the pressure levels themselves (met_p).
[in]metA pointer to a meteorological data structure containing the grid dimensions (nx, ny) and the pressure profile (pl).
[in,out]varA 3D array containing the meteorological variable to be interpolated. On output, this array will contain the interpolated values at the specified pressure levels.
[in]varnameA string representing the name of the meteorological variable being interpolated.

This function performs the following steps:

  • Sets a timer for the operation.
  • Logs the start of the interpolation process with the variable name.
  • Loops over the spatial columns (grid points).
  • For each column, copies the pressure profile.
  • Interpolates the meteorological variable to the specified pressure levels.
  • Copies the interpolated data back into the var array.
Note
The interpolation is performed in parallel using OpenMP.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 6691 of file mptrac.c.

6695 {
6696
6697 double aux[EP], p[EP];
6698
6699 /* Set timer... */
6700 SELECT_TIMER("READ_MET_ML2PL", "METPROC", NVTX_READ);
6701 LOG(2, "Interpolate meteo data to pressure levels: %s", varname);
6702
6703 /* Loop over columns... */
6704#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) private(aux,p) collapse(2)
6705 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
6706 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++) {
6707
6708 /* Copy pressure profile... */
6709 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
6710 p[ip] = met->pl[ix][iy][ip];
6711
6712 /* Interpolate... */
6713 for (int ip = 0; ip < ctl->met_np; ip++) {
6714 double pt = ctl->met_p[ip];
6715 if ((pt > p[0] && p[0] > p[1]) || (pt < p[0] && p[0] < p[1]))
6716 pt = p[0];
6717 else if ((pt > p[met->np - 1] && p[1] > p[0])
6718 || (pt < p[met->np - 1] && p[1] < p[0]))
6719 pt = p[met->np - 1];
6720 int ip2 = locate_irr(p, met->np, pt);
6721 aux[ip] = LIN(p[ip2], var[ix][iy][ip2],
6722 p[ip2 + 1], var[ix][iy][ip2 + 1], pt);
6723 }
6724
6725 /* Copy data... */
6726 for (int ip = 0; ip < ctl->met_np; ip++)
6727 var[ix][iy][ip] = (float) aux[ip];
6728 }
6729}
