8722 /* Define global attributes... */
8723 NC_PUT_ATT_GLOBAL("exp_VERTCOOR_name", "zeta");
8724 NC_PUT_ATT_GLOBAL("model", "MPTRAC");
8725
8726 /* End definitions... */
8727 NC(nc_enddef(ncid));
8728
8729 /* Write data... */
8730 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("time", atm->time, 0);
8731 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("LAT", atm->lat, 0);
8732 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("LON", atm->lon, 0);
8733 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("PRESS", atm->p, 0);
8734 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("ZETA", atm->q[ctl->qnt_zeta_d], 0);
8735 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
8736 NC_PUT_DOUBLE(ctl->qnt_name[iq], atm->q[iq], 0);
8737
8738 /* Close file... */
8739 NC(nc_close(ncid));
8740 }
8741}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ write_atm_nc()

void write_atm_nc ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const atm_t *  atm 
)

Writes air parcel data to a NetCDF file.

The write_atm_nc function creates a NetCDF file and writes air parcel data into it. The data includes time, pressure, longitude, latitude, and other specified quantities. The function defines the dimensions and variables, sets global attributes, and writes the data to the file.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure containing atmospheric data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Creates the NetCDF file with the specified filename.
  • Defines the dimension for the number of observations (obs).
  • Defines variables for time, pressure, longitude, latitude, and other quantities.
  • Sets global attributes for the feature type.
  • Writes the data into the NetCDF file.
  • Closes the NetCDF file after writing.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 8745 of file mptrac.c.

8748 {
8749
8750 int ncid, obsid, varid;
8751
8752 size_t start[2], count[2];
8753
8754 /* Create file... */
8755 NC(nc_create(filename, NC_NETCDF4, &ncid));
8756
8757 /* Define dimensions... */
8758 NC(nc_def_dim(ncid, "obs", (size_t) atm->np, &obsid));
8759
8760 /* Define variables and their attributes... */
8761 NC_DEF_VAR("time", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &obsid, "time",
8762 "seconds since 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC", ctl->atm_nc_level, 0);
8763 NC_DEF_VAR("press", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &obsid, "pressure", "hPa",
8764 ctl->atm_nc_level, 0);
8765 NC_DEF_VAR("lon", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &obsid, "longitude", "degrees_east",
8766 ctl->atm_nc_level, 0);
8767 NC_DEF_VAR("lat", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &obsid, "latitude", "degrees_north",
8768 ctl->atm_nc_level, 0);
8769 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
8770 NC_DEF_VAR(ctl->qnt_name[iq], NC_DOUBLE, 1, &obsid,
8771 ctl->qnt_longname[iq], ctl->qnt_unit[iq],
8772 ctl->atm_nc_level, ctl->atm_nc_quant[iq]);
8773
8774 /* Define global attributes... */
8775 NC_PUT_ATT_GLOBAL("featureType", "point");
8776
8777 /* End definitions... */
8778 NC(nc_enddef(ncid));
8779
8780 /* Write data... */
8781 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("time", atm->time, 0);
8782 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("press", atm->p, 0);
8783 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("lon", atm->lon, 0);
8784 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("lat", atm->lat, 0);
8785 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
8786 NC_PUT_DOUBLE(ctl->qnt_name[iq], atm->q[iq], 0);
8787
8788 /* Close file... */
8789 NC(nc_close(ncid));
8790}

◆ write_csi()

void write_csi ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes Critical Success Index (CSI) data to a file.

The write_csi function processes air parcel and observation data to calculate and write various verification statistics, including the Critical Success Index (CSI), to a specified output file at regular intervals. The statistics include measures such as the number of hits, misses, and false alarms, bias, probability of detection, false alarm rate, equitable threat score, and correlation coefficients.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure containing atmospheric data.
tA double representing the current time.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Initializes resources and sets up the output file if the current time is the start time.
  • Reads observation data and kernel data if provided.
  • Sets grid box sizes and horizontal coordinates.
  • Allocates memory for mean and count arrays.
  • Loops over observations and model data to accumulate mean values and counts.
  • Analyzes the grid cells to calculate CSI and other statistics.
  • Writes the calculated statistics to the output file at specified intervals.
  • Frees allocated resources and closes the file when the processing is complete.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 8794 of file mptrac.c.

8798 {
8799
8800 static FILE *out;
8801
8802 static double *modmean, *obsmean, *obsstd, *rt, *rz, *rlon, *rlat, *robs,
8803 *area, dlon, dlat, dz, x[NCSI], y[NCSI], obsstdn[NCSI], kz[EP], kw[EP];
8804
8805 static int *obscount, ct, cx, cy, cz, ip, ix, iy, iz, n, nobs, nk;
8806
8807 /* Set timer... */
8808 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_CSI", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
8809
8810 /* Init... */
8811 if (t == ctl->t_start) {
8812
8813 /* Check quantity index for mass... */
8814 if (ctl->qnt_m < 0)
8815 ERRMSG("Need quantity mass!");
8816
8817 /* Allocate... */
8818 ALLOC(area, double,
8819 ctl->csi_ny);
8820 ALLOC(rt, double,
8821 NOBS);
8822 ALLOC(rz, double,
8823 NOBS);
8824 ALLOC(rlon, double,
8825 NOBS);
8826 ALLOC(rlat, double,
8827 NOBS);
8828 ALLOC(robs, double,
8829 NOBS);
8830
8831 /* Read observation data... */
8832 read_obs(ctl->csi_obsfile, ctl, rt, rz, rlon, rlat, robs, &nobs);
8833
8834 /* Read kernel data... */
8835 if (ctl->csi_kernel[0] != '-')
8836 read_kernel(ctl->csi_kernel, kz, kw, &nk);
8837
8838 /* Create new file... */
8839 LOG(1, "Write CSI data: %s", filename);
8840 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
8841 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
8842
8843 /* Write header... */
8844 fprintf(out,
8845 "# $1 = time [s]\n"
8846 "# $2 = number of hits (cx)\n"
8847 "# $3 = number of misses (cy)\n"
8848 "# $4 = number of false alarms (cz)\n"
8849 "# $5 = number of observations (cx + cy)\n"
8850 "# $6 = number of forecasts (cx + cz)\n"
8851 "# $7 = bias (ratio of forecasts and observations) [%%]\n"
8852 "# $8 = probability of detection (POD) [%%]\n"
8853 "# $9 = false alarm rate (FAR) [%%]\n"
8854 "# $10 = critical success index (CSI) [%%]\n");
8855 fprintf(out,
8856 "# $11 = hits associated with random chance\n"
8857 "# $12 = equitable threat score (ETS) [%%]\n"
8858 "# $13 = Pearson linear correlation coefficient\n"
8859 "# $14 = Spearman rank-order correlation coefficient\n"
8860 "# $15 = column density mean error (F - O) [kg/m^2]\n"
8861 "# $16 = column density root mean square error (RMSE) [kg/m^2]\n"
8862 "# $17 = column density mean absolute error [kg/m^2]\n"
8863 "# $18 = log-likelihood function\n"
8864 "# $19 = number of data points\n\n");
8865
8866 /* Set grid box size... */
8867 dz = (ctl->csi_z1 - ctl->csi_z0) / ctl->csi_nz;
8868 dlon = (ctl->csi_lon1 - ctl->csi_lon0) / ctl->csi_nx;
8869 dlat = (ctl->csi_lat1 - ctl->csi_lat0) / ctl->csi_ny;
8870
8871 /* Set horizontal coordinates... */
8872 for (iy = 0; iy < ctl->csi_ny; iy++) {
8873 const double lat = ctl->csi_lat0 + dlat * (iy + 0.5);
8874 area[iy] = dlat * dlon * SQR(RE * M_PI / 180.) * cos(DEG2RAD(lat));
8875 }
8876 }
8877
8878 /* Set time interval... */
8879 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
8880 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
8881
8882 /* Allocate... */
8883 ALLOC(modmean, double,
8884 ctl->csi_nx * ctl->csi_ny * ctl->csi_nz);
8885 ALLOC(obsmean, double,
8886 ctl->csi_nx * ctl->csi_ny * ctl->csi_nz);
8887 ALLOC(obscount, int,
8888 ctl->csi_nx * ctl->csi_ny * ctl->csi_nz);
8889 ALLOC(obsstd, double,
8890 ctl->csi_nx * ctl->csi_ny * ctl->csi_nz);
8891
8892 /* Loop over observations... */
8893 for (int i = 0; i < nobs; i++) {
8894
8895 /* Check time... */
8896 if (rt[i] < t0)
8897 continue;
8898 else if (rt[i] >= t1)
8899 break;
8900
8901 /* Check observation data... */
8902 if (!isfinite(robs[i]))
8903 continue;
8904
8905 /* Calculate indices... */
8906 ix = (int) ((rlon[i] - ctl->csi_lon0) / dlon);
8907 iy = (int) ((rlat[i] - ctl->csi_lat0) / dlat);
8908 iz = (int) ((rz[i] - ctl->csi_z0) / dz);
8909
8910 /* Check indices... */
8911 if (ix < 0 || ix >= ctl->csi_nx ||
8912 iy < 0 || iy >= ctl->csi_ny || iz < 0 || iz >= ctl->csi_nz)
8913 continue;
8914
8915 /* Get mean observation index... */
8916 int idx = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->csi_ny, iz, ctl->csi_nz);
8917 obsmean[idx] += robs[i];
8918 obsstd[idx] += SQR(robs[i]);
8919 obscount[idx]++;
8920 }
8921
8922 /* Analyze model data... */
8923 for (ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++) {
8924
8925 /* Check time... */
8926 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
8927 continue;
8928
8929 /* Get indices... */
8930 ix = (int) ((atm->lon[ip] - ctl->csi_lon0) / dlon);
8931 iy = (int) ((atm->lat[ip] - ctl->csi_lat0) / dlat);
8932 iz = (int) ((Z(atm->p[ip]) - ctl->csi_z0) / dz);
8933
8934 /* Check indices... */
8935 if (ix < 0 || ix >= ctl->csi_nx ||
8936 iy < 0 || iy >= ctl->csi_ny || iz < 0 || iz >= ctl->csi_nz)
8937 continue;
8938
8939 /* Get total mass in grid cell... */
8940 int idx = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->csi_ny, iz, ctl->csi_nz);
8941 modmean[idx] += kernel_weight(kz, kw, nk, atm->p[ip])
8942 * atm->q[ctl->qnt_m][ip];
8943 }
8944
8945 /* Analyze all grid cells... */
8946 for (ix = 0; ix < ctl->csi_nx; ix++)
8947 for (iy = 0; iy < ctl->csi_ny; iy++)
8948 for (iz = 0; iz < ctl->csi_nz; iz++) {
8949
8950 /* Calculate mean observation index... */
8951 int idx = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->csi_ny, iz, ctl->csi_nz);
8952 if (obscount[idx] > 0) {
8953 obsmean[idx] /= obscount[idx];
8954 obsstd[idx] -= SQR(obsmean[idx]);
8955 obsstd[idx] = sqrt(obsstd[idx]);
8956 }
8957
8958 /* Calculate column density... */
8959 if (modmean[idx] > 0)
8960 modmean[idx] /= (1e6 * area[iy]);
8961
8962 /* Calculate CSI... */
8963 if (obscount[idx] > 0) {
8964 ct++;
8965 if (obsmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_obsmin &&
8966 modmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_modmin)
8967 cx++;
8968 else if (obsmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_obsmin &&
8969 modmean[idx] < ctl->csi_modmin)
8970 cy++;
8971 else if (obsmean[idx] < ctl->csi_obsmin &&
8972 modmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_modmin)
8973 cz++;
8974 }
8975
8976 /* Save data for other verification statistics... */
8977 if (obscount[idx] > 0
8978 && (obsmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_obsmin
8979 || modmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_modmin)) {
8980 x[n] = modmean[idx];
8981 y[n] = obsmean[idx];
8982 if (modmean[idx] >= ctl->csi_modmin)
8983 obsstdn[n] = obsstd[idx];
8984 if ((++n) >= NCSI)
8985 ERRMSG("Too many data points to calculate statistics!");
8986 }
8987 }
8988
8989 /* Write output... */
8990 if (fmod(t, ctl->csi_dt_out) == 0) {
8991
8992 /* Calculate verification statistics
8993 (https://www.cawcr.gov.au/projects/verification/) ... */
8994 static double work[2 * NCSI], work2[2 * NCSI];;
8995 const int n_obs = cx + cy;
8996 const int n_for = cx + cz;
8997 const double bias = (n_obs > 0) ? 100. * n_for / n_obs : NAN;
8998 const double pod = (n_obs > 0) ? (100. * cx) / n_obs : NAN;
8999 const double far = (n_for > 0) ? (100. * cz) / n_for : NAN;
9000 const double csi =
9001 (cx + cy + cz > 0) ? (100. * cx) / (cx + cy + cz) : NAN;
9002 const double cx_rd = (ct > 0) ? (1. * n_obs * n_for) / ct : NAN;
9003 const double ets = (cx + cy + cz - cx_rd > 0) ?
9004 (100. * (cx - cx_rd)) / (cx + cy + cz - cx_rd) : NAN;
9005 const double rho_p =
9006 (n > 0) ? gsl_stats_correlation(x, 1, y, 1, (size_t) n) : NAN;
9007 const double rho_s =
9008 (n > 0) ? gsl_stats_spearman(x, 1, y, 1, (size_t) n, work) : NAN;
9009 for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
9010 work[i] = x[i] - y[i];
9011 work2[i] = (obsstdn[i] != 0) ? (x[i] - y[i]) / obsstdn[i] : 0;
9012 }
9013 const double mean = (n > 0) ? gsl_stats_mean(work, 1, (size_t) n) : NAN;
9014 const double rmse =
9015 (n > 0) ? gsl_stats_sd_with_fixed_mean(work, 1, (size_t) n,
9016 0.0) : NAN;
9017 const double absdev =
9018 (n > 0) ? gsl_stats_absdev_m(work, 1, (size_t) n, 0.0) : NAN;
9019 const double loglikelihood =
9020 (n > 0) ? gsl_stats_tss(work2, 1, (size_t) n) * (-0.5) : GSL_NAN;
9021
9022 /* Write... */
9023 fprintf(out,
9024 "%.2f %d %d %d %d %d %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %d\n", t,
9025 cx, cy, cz, n_obs, n_for, bias, pod, far, csi, cx_rd, ets, rho_p,
9026 rho_s, mean, rmse, absdev, loglikelihood, n);
9027
9028 /* Set counters to zero... */
9029 n = ct = cx = cy = cz = 0;
9030 }
9031
9032 /* Free... */
9033 free(modmean);
9034 free(obsmean);
9035 free(obscount);
9036 free(obsstd);
9037
9038 /* Finalize... */
9039 if (t == ctl->t_stop) {
9040
9041 /* Close output file... */
9042 fclose(out);
9043
9044 /* Free... */
9045 free(area);
9046 free(rt);
9047 free(rz);
9048 free(rlon);
9049 free(rlat);
9050 free(robs);
9051 }
9052}

◆ write_ens()

void write_ens ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes ensemble data to a file.

The write_ens function processes air parcel data to calculate ensemble means and standard deviations for various quantities and writes them to a specified output file. It handles ensemble members and calculates statistics such as means and standard deviations for each ensemble, along with latitude, longitude, altitude, and time information.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure containing atmospheric data.
tA double representing the current time.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Initializes resources and sets up necessary variables.
  • Sets a time interval for processing data.
  • Loops over air parcels to accumulate means and standard deviations for each ensemble member.
  • Creates an output file and writes header information.
  • Writes ensemble data, including time, altitude, latitude, longitude, means, standard deviations, and the number of members.
  • Closes the output file.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9056 of file mptrac.c.

9060 {
9061
9062 static FILE *out;
9063
9064 static double dummy, lat, lon, qm[NQ][NENS], qs[NQ][NENS], xm[NENS][3],
9065 x[3], zm[NENS];
9066
9067 static int n[NENS];
9068
9069 /* Set timer... */
9070 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_ENS", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
9071
9072 /* Check quantities... */
9073 if (ctl->qnt_ens < 0)
9074 ERRMSG("Missing ensemble IDs!");
9075
9076 /* Set time interval... */
9077 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
9078 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
9079
9080 /* Init... */
9081 for (int i = 0; i < NENS; i++) {
9082 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
9083 qm[iq][i] = qs[iq][i] = 0;
9084 xm[i][0] = xm[i][1] = xm[i][2] = zm[i] = 0;
9085 n[i] = 0;
9086 }
9087
9088 /* Loop over air parcels... */
9089 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++) {
9090
9091 /* Check time... */
9092 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
9093 continue;
9094
9095 /* Check ensemble ID... */
9096 if (atm->q[ctl->qnt_ens][ip] < 0 || atm->q[ctl->qnt_ens][ip] >= NENS)
9097 ERRMSG("Ensemble ID is out of range!");
9098
9099 /* Get means... */
9100 geo2cart(0, atm->lon[ip], atm->lat[ip], x);
9101 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9102 qm[iq][ctl->qnt_ens] += atm->q[iq][ip];
9103 qs[iq][ctl->qnt_ens] += SQR(atm->q[iq][ip]);
9104 }
9105 xm[ctl->qnt_ens][0] += x[0];
9106 xm[ctl->qnt_ens][1] += x[1];
9107 xm[ctl->qnt_ens][2] += x[2];
9108 zm[ctl->qnt_ens] += Z(atm->p[ip]);
9109 n[ctl->qnt_ens]++;
9110 }
9111
9112 /* Create file... */
9113 LOG(1, "Write ensemble data: %s", filename);
9114 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
9115 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
9116
9117 /* Write header... */
9118 fprintf(out,
9119 "# $1 = time [s]\n"
9120 "# $2 = altitude [km]\n"
9121 "# $3 = longitude [deg]\n" "# $4 = latitude [deg]\n");
9122 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
9123 fprintf(out, "# $%d = %s (mean) [%s]\n", 5 + iq,
9124 ctl->qnt_name[iq], ctl->qnt_unit[iq]);
9125 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
9126 fprintf(out, "# $%d = %s (sigma) [%s]\n", 5 + ctl->nq + iq,
9127 ctl->qnt_name[iq], ctl->qnt_unit[iq]);
9128 fprintf(out, "# $%d = number of members\n\n", 5 + 2 * ctl->nq);
9129
9130 /* Write data... */
9131 for (int i = 0; i < NENS; i++)
9132 if (n[i] > 0) {
9133 cart2geo(xm[i], &dummy, &lon, &lat);
9134 fprintf(out, "%.2f %g %g %g", t, zm[i] / n[i], lon, lat);
9135 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9136 fprintf(out, " ");
9137 fprintf(out, ctl->qnt_format[iq], qm[iq][i] / n[i]);
9138 }
9139 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9140 fprintf(out, " ");
9141 double var = qs[iq][i] / n[i] - SQR(qm[iq][i] / n[i]);
9142 fprintf(out, ctl->qnt_format[iq], (var > 0 ? sqrt(var) : 0));
9143 }
9144 fprintf(out, " %d\n", n[i]);
9145 }
9146
9147 /* Close file... */
9148 fclose(out);
9149}

◆ write_grid()

void write_grid ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met0,
met_t *  met1,
const atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes grid data to a file in ASCII or netCDF format.

The write_grid function processes air parcel data to calculate various grid-based statistics such as column density, mean, and standard deviation for specified quantities. It then writes this data to a specified output file either in ASCII or netCDF format based on the configuration parameters provided in the ctl structure.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
met0A pointer to a met_t structure containing meteorological data for the initial time step.
met1A pointer to a met_t structure containing meteorological data for the final time step.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure containing atmospheric data.
tA double representing the current time.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Initializes resources and sets up necessary variables.
  • Reads kernel data if it is specified in the control parameters.
  • Allocates memory for various arrays to store grid data.
  • Determines the grid box size and sets up vertical and horizontal coordinates.
  • Sets a time interval for output data processing.
  • Calculates grid box indices for atmospheric model data.
  • Averages data within each grid box.
  • Calculates column density and volume mixing ratio.
  • Writes data to the output file either in ASCII or netCDF format based on the specified grid_type in the control parameters.
  • Frees allocated memory.
Note
The function supports parallel processing using OpenMP for certain computational tasks to improve performance.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9153 of file mptrac.c.

9159 {
9160
9161 static double kz[EP], kw[EP];
9162
9163 static int nk;
9164
9165 double *cd, *mean[NQ], *sigma[NQ], *vmr_impl, *z, *lon, *lat, *area, *press;
9166
9167 int *ixs, *iys, *izs, *np;
9168
9169 /* Set timer... */
9170 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_GRID", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
9171
9172 /* Write info... */
9173 LOG(1, "Write grid data: %s", filename);
9174
9175 /* Init... */
9176 if (t == ctl->t_start) {
9177
9178 /* Read kernel data... */
9179 if (ctl->grid_kernel[0] != '-')
9180 read_kernel(ctl->grid_kernel, kz, kw, &nk);
9181 }
9182
9183 /* Allocate... */
9184 ALLOC(cd, double,
9185 ctl->grid_nx * ctl->grid_ny * ctl->grid_nz);
9186 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9187 ALLOC(mean[iq], double,
9188 ctl->grid_nx * ctl->grid_ny * ctl->grid_nz);
9189 ALLOC(sigma[iq], double,
9190 ctl->grid_nx * ctl->grid_ny * ctl->grid_nz);
9191 }
9192 ALLOC(vmr_impl, double,
9193 ctl->grid_nx * ctl->grid_ny * ctl->grid_nz);
9194 ALLOC(z, double,
9195 ctl->grid_nz);
9196 ALLOC(lon, double,
9197 ctl->grid_nx);
9198 ALLOC(lat, double,
9199 ctl->grid_ny);
9200 ALLOC(area, double,
9201 ctl->grid_ny);
9202 ALLOC(press, double,
9203 ctl->grid_nz);
9204 ALLOC(np, int,
9205 ctl->grid_nx * ctl->grid_ny * ctl->grid_nz);
9206 ALLOC(ixs, int,
9207 atm->np);
9208 ALLOC(iys, int,
9209 atm->np);
9210 ALLOC(izs, int,
9211 atm->np);
9212
9213 /* Set grid box size... */
9214 const double dz = (ctl->grid_z1 - ctl->grid_z0) / ctl->grid_nz;
9215 const double dlon = (ctl->grid_lon1 - ctl->grid_lon0) / ctl->grid_nx;
9216 const double dlat = (ctl->grid_lat1 - ctl->grid_lat0) / ctl->grid_ny;
9217
9218 /* Set vertical coordinates... */
9219#pragma omp parallel for default(shared)
9220 for (int iz = 0; iz < ctl->grid_nz; iz++) {
9221 z[iz] = ctl->grid_z0 + dz * (iz + 0.5);
9222 press[iz] = P(z[iz]);
9223 }
9224
9225 /* Set horizontal coordinates... */
9226 for (int ix = 0; ix < ctl->grid_nx; ix++)
9227 lon[ix] = ctl->grid_lon0 + dlon * (ix + 0.5);
9228#pragma omp parallel for default(shared)
9229 for (int iy = 0; iy < ctl->grid_ny; iy++) {
9230 lat[iy] = ctl->grid_lat0 + dlat * (iy + 0.5);
9231 area[iy] = dlat * dlon * SQR(RE * M_PI / 180.) * cos(DEG2RAD(lat[iy]));
9232 }
9233
9234 /* Set time interval for output... */
9235 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
9236 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
9237
9238 /* Get grid box indices... */
9239#pragma omp parallel for default(shared)
9240 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++) {
9241 ixs[ip] = (int) ((atm->lon[ip] - ctl->grid_lon0) / dlon);
9242 iys[ip] = (int) ((atm->lat[ip] - ctl->grid_lat0) / dlat);
9243 izs[ip] = (int) ((Z(atm->p[ip]) - ctl->grid_z0) / dz);
9244 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1
9245 || ixs[ip] < 0 || ixs[ip] >= ctl->grid_nx
9246 || iys[ip] < 0 || iys[ip] >= ctl->grid_ny
9247 || izs[ip] < 0 || izs[ip] >= ctl->grid_nz)
9248 izs[ip] = -1;
9249 }
9250
9251 /* Average data... */
9252 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++)
9253 if (izs[ip] >= 0) {
9254 int idx =
9255 ARRAY_3D(ixs[ip], iys[ip], ctl->grid_ny, izs[ip], ctl->grid_nz);
9256 double kernel = kernel_weight(kz, kw, nk, atm->p[ip]);
9257 np[idx]++;
9258 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9259 mean[iq][idx] += kernel * atm->q[iq][ip];
9260 sigma[iq][idx] += SQR(kernel * atm->q[iq][ip]);
9261 }
9262 }
9263
9264 /* Calculate column density and volume mixing ratio... */
9265#pragma omp parallel for default(shared)
9266 for (int ix = 0; ix < ctl->grid_nx; ix++)
9267 for (int iy = 0; iy < ctl->grid_ny; iy++)
9268 for (int iz = 0; iz < ctl->grid_nz; iz++) {
9269
9270 /* Get grid index... */
9271 int idx = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->grid_ny, iz, ctl->grid_nz);
9272
9273 /* Calculate column density... */
9274 cd[idx] = NAN;
9275 if (ctl->qnt_m >= 0)
9276 cd[idx] = mean[ctl->qnt_m][idx] / (1e6 * area[iy]);
9277
9278 /* Calculate volume mixing ratio (implicit)... */
9279 vmr_impl[idx] = NAN;
9280 if (ctl->qnt_m >= 0 && ctl->molmass > 0 && met0 != NULL
9281 && met1 != NULL) {
9282 vmr_impl[idx] = 0;
9283 if (mean[ctl->qnt_m][idx] > 0) {
9284
9285 /* Get temperature... */
9286 double temp;
9287 INTPOL_INIT;
9288 intpol_met_time_3d(met0, met0->t, met1, met1->t, t, press[iz],
9289 lon[ix], lat[iy], &temp, ci, cw, 1);
9290
9291 /* Calculate volume mixing ratio... */
9292 vmr_impl[idx] = MA / ctl->molmass * mean[ctl->qnt_m][idx]
9293 / (RHO(press[iz], temp) * 1e6 * area[iy] * 1e3 * dz);
9294 }
9295 }
9296
9297 /* Calculate mean... */
9298 if (np[idx] > 0)
9299 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9300 mean[iq][idx] /= np[idx];
9301 double var = sigma[iq][idx] / np[idx] - SQR(mean[iq][idx]);
9302 sigma[iq][idx] = (var > 0 ? sqrt(var) : 0);
9303 } else
9304 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9305 mean[iq][idx] = NAN;
9306 sigma[iq][idx] = NAN;
9307 }
9308 }
9309
9310 /* Write ASCII data... */
9311 if (ctl->grid_type == 0)
9312 write_grid_asc(filename, ctl, cd, mean, sigma, vmr_impl,
9313 t, z, lon, lat, area, dz, np);
9314
9315 /* Write netCDF data... */
9316 else if (ctl->grid_type == 1)
9317 write_grid_nc(filename, ctl, cd, mean, sigma, vmr_impl,
9318 t, z, lon, lat, area, dz, np);
9319
9320 /* Error message... */
9321 else
9322 ERRMSG("Grid data format GRID_TYPE unknown!");
9323
9324 /* Free... */
9325 free(cd);
9326 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
9327 free(mean[iq]);
9328 free(sigma[iq]);
9329 }
9330 free(vmr_impl);
9331 free(z);
9332 free(lon);
9333 free(lat);
9334 free(area);
9335 free(press);
9336 free(np);
9337 free(ixs);
9338 free(iys);
9339 free(izs);
9340}

◆ write_grid_asc()

void write_grid_asc ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const double *  cd,
double *  mean[NQ],
double *  sigma[NQ],
const double *  vmr_impl,
const double  t,
const double *  z,
const double *  lon,
const double *  lat,
const double *  area,
const double  dz,
const int *  np 
)

Writes grid data to an ASCII file.

The write_grid_asc function writes gridded air parcel data, including column density, mean and standard deviation for specified quantities, and volume mixing ratio (if available), to an ASCII file. The function also supports writing gnuplot commands to generate plots if requested in the control parameters.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
cdAn array of doubles representing column density values.
meanAn array of arrays of doubles representing the mean values for specified quantities.
sigmaAn array of arrays of doubles representing the standard deviation values for specified quantities.
vmr_implAn array of doubles representing the volume mixing ratio (implicit) values.
tA double representing the current time.
zAn array of doubles representing vertical coordinates (altitude).
lonAn array of doubles representing longitudinal coordinates.
latAn array of doubles representing latitudinal coordinates.
areaAn array of doubles representing surface area values.
dzA double representing the layer depth.
npAn array of integers representing the number of particles.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Checks if gnuplot output is requested in the control parameters and sets up a gnuplot pipe if needed.
  • If gnuplot output is requested, sets the plot filename and time string, and dumps gnuplot file contents to the pipe.
  • Otherwise, creates the output file for writing in ASCII format.
  • Writes the header information to the output file, including column labels.
  • Writes the grid data to the output file, including time, altitude, coordinates, surface area, layer depth, column density, volume mixing ratio, number of particles, mean values for specified quantities, and standard deviation values if requested.
  • Closes the output file.
Note
The function supports writing gnuplot commands to generate plots if requested in the control parameters. It also supports writing mean and standard deviation values for specified quantities if requested.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9344 of file mptrac.c.

9363
9364 /* Create gnuplot pipe... */
9365 if (!(out = popen("gnuplot", "w")))
9366 ERRMSG("Cannot create pipe to gnuplot!");
9367
9368 /* Set plot filename... */

9370

9374 jsec2time(t, &year, &mon, &day, &hour, &min, &sec, &r);
9375 fprintf(out, "timestr=\"%d-%02d-%02d, %02d:%02d UTC\"\n",
9376 year, mon, day, hour, min);
9377
9378 /* Dump gnuplot file to pipe... */

9380 char line[LEN];
9381 if (!(in = fopen(ctl->grid_gpfile, "r")))
9382 ERRMSG("Cannot open file!");
9383 while (fgets(line, LEN, in))
9384 fprintf(out, "%s", line);
9385 fclose(in);
9386 }
9387
9388 else {
9389
9390 /* Create file... */
9391 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
9392 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
9393 }
9394
9395 /* Write header... */
9396 fprintf(out,
9397 "# $1 = time [s]\n"
9398 "# $2 = altitude [km]\n"
9399 "# $3 = longitude [deg]\n"
9400 "# $4 = latitude [deg]\n"
9401 "# $5 = surface area [km^2]\n"
9402 "# $6 = layer depth [km]\n"
9403 "# $7 = column density (implicit) [kg/m^2]\n"
9404 "# $8 = volume mixing ratio (implicit) [ppv]\n"
9405 "# $9 = number of particles [1]\n");
9406 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
9407 fprintf(out, "# $%i = %s (mean) [%s]\n", 10 + iq, ctl->qnt_name[iq],
9408 ctl->qnt_unit[iq]);
9409 if (ctl->grid_stddev)
9410 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
9411 fprintf(out, "# $%i = %s (stddev) [%s]\n", 10 + ctl->nq + iq,
9412 ctl->qnt_name[iq], ctl->qnt_unit[iq]);
9413 fprintf(out, "\n");
9414
9415 /* Write data... */
9416 for (int ix = 0; ix < ctl->grid_nx; ix++) {

9419 for (int iy = 0; iy < ctl->grid_ny; iy++) {

9425 fprintf(out, "%.2f %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %d", t, z[iz], lon[ix],
9426 lat[iy], area[iy], dz, cd[idx], vmr_impl[idx], np[idx]);
9427 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {

9430 }

9435 }
9436 fprintf(out, "\n");
9437 }

9440 }
9441
9442 /* Close file... */
9443 fclose(out);

◆ write_grid_nc()

void write_grid_nc ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const double *  cd,
double *  mean[NQ],
double *  sigma[NQ],
const double *  vmr_impl,
const double  t,
const double *  z,
const double *  lon,
const double *  lat,
const double *  area,
const double  dz,
const int *  np 
)

Writes grid data to a NetCDF file.

The write_grid_nc function writes gridded air parcel data, including column density, mean and standard deviation for specified quantities, and volume mixing ratio (if available), to a NetCDF file. NetCDF is a self-describing, machine-independent data format for storing scientific data.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
cdAn array of doubles representing column density values.
meanAn array of arrays of doubles representing the mean values for specified quantities.
sigmaAn array of arrays of doubles representing the standard deviation values for specified quantities.
vmr_implAn array of doubles representing the volume mixing ratio (implicit) values.
tA double representing the current time.
zAn array of doubles representing vertical coordinates (altitude).
lonAn array of doubles representing longitudinal coordinates.
latAn array of doubles representing latitudinal coordinates.
areaAn array of doubles representing surface area values.
dzA double representing the layer depth.
npAn array of integers representing the number of particles.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Allocates memory for temporary arrays required for writing data.
  • Creates a NetCDF file with the specified filename.
  • Defines dimensions and variables in the NetCDF file, along with their attributes.
  • Writes the data arrays to the NetCDF file.
  • Closes the NetCDF file.
  • Frees allocated memory.
Note
NetCDF files are commonly used in scientific computing and can be accessed by various programming languages and software packages. Additionally, the function supports writing mean and standard deviation values for specified quantities if requested.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9448 of file mptrac.c.

NC_PUT_INT
#define NC_PUT_INT(varname, ptr, hyperslab)

Definition: mptrac.h:1155

◆ write_met()

int write_met ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met 
)

Writes meteorological data to a binary file.

The write_met function writes meteorological data, including surface data and level data, to a binary file specified by the filename parameter. The data is written in binary format for efficient storage and transfer.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the name of the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
metA pointer to a met_t structure containing meteorological data.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets a timer to measure the execution time.
  • Writes information about the meteorological data being written to the log.
  • Checks if compression flags are enabled and displays error messages if required compression methods are not available.
  • Writes binary data to the file, including the type and version of the data, grid data (time, dimensions, coordinates), surface data, and level data.
  • Closes the file after writing all data.
Returns
Returns an integer value indicating the success or failure of the function. A value of 0 indicates successful execution.
Note
This function supports writing meteorological data in different binary formats, including uncompressed and compressed formats using various compression algorithms such as packing, ZFP, ZSTD, and cmultiscale. The specific compression method used depends on the configuration specified in the ctl_t structure.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9578 of file mptrac.c.

9581 {
9582
9583 /* Set timer... */
9584 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_MET", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
9585
9586 /* Write info... */
9587 LOG(1, "Write meteo data: %s", filename);
9588
9589 /* Check compression flags... */
9590#ifndef ZFP
9591 if (ctl->met_type == 3)
9592 ERRMSG("MPTRAC was compiled without zfp compression!");
9593#endif
9594#ifndef ZSTD
9595 if (ctl->met_type == 4)
9596 ERRMSG("MPTRAC was compiled without zstd compression!");
9597#endif
9598#ifndef CMS
9599 if (ctl->met_type == 5)
9600 ERRMSG("MPTRAC was compiled without cmultiscale compression!");
9601#endif
9602
9603 /* Write netCDF data... */
9604 if (ctl->met_type == 0) {
9605
9606 /* Create file... */
9607 int ncid, varid;
9608 size_t start[4], count[4];
9609 nc_create(filename, NC_NETCDF4, &ncid);
9610
9611 /* Define dimensions... */
9612 int tid, lonid, latid, levid;
9613 NC(nc_def_dim(ncid, "time", 1, &tid));
9614 NC(nc_def_dim(ncid, "lon", (size_t) met->nx, &lonid));
9615 NC(nc_def_dim(ncid, "lat", (size_t) met->ny, &latid));
9616 NC(nc_def_dim(ncid, "lev", (size_t) met->np, &levid));
9617
9618 /* Define grid... */
9619 NC_DEF_VAR("time", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &tid, "time",
9620 "seconds since 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC", 0, 0);
9621 NC_DEF_VAR("lon", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &lonid, "longitude", "degrees_east", 0,
9622 0);
9623 NC_DEF_VAR("lat", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &latid, "latitude", "degrees_north", 0,
9624 0);
9625 NC_DEF_VAR("lev", NC_DOUBLE, 1, &levid, "pressure", "Pa", 0, 0);
9626
9627 /* Define surface variables... */
9628 int dimid2[2] = { latid, lonid };
9629 NC_DEF_VAR("sp", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "Surface pressure", "Pa",
9630 ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9631 NC_DEF_VAR("z", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "Geopotential", "m**2 s**-2",
9632 ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9633 NC_DEF_VAR("t2m", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "2 metre temperature", "K",
9634 ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9635 NC_DEF_VAR("u10m", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "10 metre U wind component",
9636 "m s**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9637 NC_DEF_VAR("v10m", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "10 metre V wind component",
9638 "m s**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9639 NC_DEF_VAR("lsm", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "Land/sea mask", "-",
9640 ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9641 NC_DEF_VAR("sstk", NC_FLOAT, 2, dimid2, "Sea surface temperature", "K",
9642 ctl->met_nc_level, 0);
9643
9644 /* Define level data... */
9645 int dimid3[3] = { levid, latid, lonid };
9646 NC_DEF_VAR("t", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Temperature", "K",
9647 ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9648 NC_DEF_VAR("u", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "U velocity", "m s**-1",
9649 ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9650 NC_DEF_VAR("v", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "V velocity", "m s**-1",
9651 ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9652 NC_DEF_VAR("w", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Vertical velocity", "Pa s**-1",
9653 ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9654 NC_DEF_VAR("q", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Specific humidity", "kg kg**-1",
9655 ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9656 NC_DEF_VAR("o3", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Ozone mass mixing ratio",
9657 "kg kg**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9658 NC_DEF_VAR("clwc", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Cloud liquid water content",
9659 "kg kg**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9660 NC_DEF_VAR("crwc", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Cloud rain water content",
9661 "kg kg**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9662 NC_DEF_VAR("ciwc", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Cloud ice water content",
9663 "kg kg**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9664 NC_DEF_VAR("cswc", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Cloud snow water content",
9665 "kg kg**-1", ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9666 NC_DEF_VAR("cc", NC_FLOAT, 3, dimid3, "Cloud cover", "-",
9667 ctl->met_nc_level, ctl->met_nc_quant);
9668
9669 /* End definitions... */
9670 NC(nc_enddef(ncid));
9671
9672 /* Write grid data... */
9673 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("time", &met->time, 0);
9674 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("lon", met->lon, 0);
9675 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("lat", met->lat, 0);
9676 double phelp[EP];
9677 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
9678 phelp[ip] = 100. * met->p[ip];
9679 NC_PUT_DOUBLE("lev", phelp, 0);
9680
9681 /* Write surface data... */
9682 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "sp", met, met->ps, 100.0f);
9683 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "z", met, met->zs, (float) (1000. * G0));
9684 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "t2m", met, met->ts, 1.0f);
9685 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "u10m", met, met->us, 1.0f);
9686 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "v10m", met, met->vs, 1.0f);
9687 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "lsm", met, met->lsm, 1.0f);
9688 write_met_nc_2d(ncid, "sstk", met, met->sst, 1.0f);
9689
9690 /* Write level data... */
9691 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "t", met, met->t, 1.0f);
9692 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "u", met, met->u, 1.0f);
9693 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "v", met, met->v, 1.0f);
9694 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "w", met, met->w, 100.0f);
9695 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "q", met, met->h2o, (float) (MH2O / MA));
9696 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "o3", met, met->o3, (float) (MO3 / MA));
9697 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "clwc", met, met->lwc, 1.0f);
9698 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "crwc", met, met->rwc, 1.0f);
9699 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "ciwc", met, met->iwc, 1.0f);
9700 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "cswc", met, met->swc, 1.0f);
9701 write_met_nc_3d(ncid, "cc", met, met->cc, 1.0f);
9702
9703 /* Close file... */
9704 NC(nc_close(ncid));
9705 }
9706
9707 /* Write binary data... */
9708 else if (ctl->met_type >= 1 && ctl->met_type <= 5) {
9709
9710 /* Create file... */
9711 FILE *out;
9712 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
9713 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
9714
9715 /* Write type of binary data... */
9716 FWRITE(&ctl->met_type, int,
9717 1,
9718 out);
9719
9720 /* Write version of binary data... */
9721 int version = 102;
9722 FWRITE(&version, int,
9723 1,
9724 out);
9725
9726 /* Write grid data... */
9727 FWRITE(&met->time, double,
9728 1,
9729 out);
9730 FWRITE(&met->nx, int,
9731 1,
9732 out);
9733 FWRITE(&met->ny, int,
9734 1,
9735 out);
9736 FWRITE(&met->np, int,
9737 1,
9738 out);
9739 FWRITE(met->lon, double,
9740 (size_t) met->nx,
9741 out);
9742 FWRITE(met->lat, double,
9743 (size_t) met->ny,
9744 out);
9745 FWRITE(met->p, double,
9746 (size_t) met->np,
9747 out);
9748
9749 /* Write surface data... */
9750 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->ps, "PS");
9751 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->ts, "TS");
9752 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->zs, "ZS");
9753 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->us, "US");
9754 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->vs, "VS");
9755 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->lsm, "LSM");
9756 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->sst, "SST");
9757 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->pbl, "PBL");
9758 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->pt, "PT");
9759 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->tt, "TT");
9760 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->zt, "ZT");
9761 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->h2ot, "H2OT");
9762 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->pct, "PCT");
9763 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->pcb, "PCB");
9764 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->cl, "CL");
9765 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->plcl, "PLCL");
9766 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->plfc, "PLFC");
9767 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->pel, "PEL");
9768 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->cape, "CAPE");
9769 write_met_bin_2d(out, met, met->cin, "CIN");
9770
9771 /* Write level data... */
9772 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->z, "Z",
9773 (ctl->met_zfp_tol_z <= 0 ? ctl->met_zfp_prec : 0),
9774 ctl->met_zfp_tol_z);
9775 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->t, "T",
9776 (ctl->met_zfp_tol_t <= 0 ? ctl->met_zfp_prec : 0),
9777 ctl->met_zfp_tol_t);
9778 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->u, "U", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9779 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->v, "V", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9780 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->w, "W", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9781 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->pv, "PV", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9782 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->h2o, "H2O", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9783 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->o3, "O3", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9784 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->lwc, "LWC", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9785 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->rwc, "RWC", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9786 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->iwc, "IWC", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9787 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->swc, "SWC", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9788 write_met_bin_3d(out, ctl, met, met->cc, "CC", ctl->met_zfp_prec, 0);
9789
9790 /* Write final flag... */
9791 int final = 999;
9792 FWRITE(&final, int,
9793 1,
9794 out);
9795
9796 /* Close file... */
9797 fclose(out);
9798 }
9799
9800 /* Not implemented... */
9801 else
9802 ERRMSG("MET_TYPE not implemented!");
9803
9804 return 0;
9805}

◆ write_met_bin_2d()

void write_met_bin_2d ( FILE *  out,
met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY],
const char *  varname 
)

Writes a 2-dimensional meteorological variable to a binary file.

The write_met_bin_2d function writes a 2-dimensional meteorological variable to a binary file specified by the out parameter. The variable data is provided in a 2-dimensional array var with maximum dimensions EX by EY. The variable name is provided as a string in the varname parameter.

Parameters
outA pointer to a FILE structure representing the output file.
metA pointer to a met_t structure containing meteorological data.
varAn array of floats representing the 2-dimensional variable data.
varnameA string containing the name of the variable being written.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Allocates memory for a temporary buffer to hold the variable data.
  • Copies the variable data from the 2-dimensional array var to the temporary buffer help.
  • Writes the uncompressed variable data to the binary file specified by out.
  • Logs a message indicating the successful writing of the variable data.
  • Frees the allocated memory.
Note
This function is typically used to write surface data or other 2-dimensional meteorological variables to a binary file.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9809 of file mptrac.c.

9813 {
9814
9815 float *help;
9816
9817 /* Allocate... */
9818 ALLOC(help, float,
9819 EX * EY);
9820
9821 /* Copy data... */
9822 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
9823 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
9824 help[ARRAY_2D(ix, iy, met->ny)] = var[ix][iy];
9825
9826 /* Write uncompressed data... */
9827 LOG(2, "Write 2-D variable: %s (uncompressed)", varname);
9828 FWRITE(help, float,
9829 (size_t) (met->nx * met->ny),
9830 out);
9831
9832 /* Free... */
9833 free(help);
9834}

◆ write_met_bin_3d()

void write_met_bin_3d ( FILE *  out,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY][EP],
const char *  varname,
const int  precision,
const double  tolerance 
)

Writes a 3-dimensional meteorological variable to a binary file.

The write_met_bin_3d function writes a 3-dimensional meteorological variable to a binary file specified by the out parameter. The variable data is provided in a 3-dimensional array var with maximum dimensions EX by EY by EP. The variable name is provided as a string in the varname parameter. Additionally, the function takes parameters for specifying the compression precision and tolerance.

Parameters
outA pointer to a FILE structure representing the output file.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
metA pointer to a met_t structure containing meteorological data.
varAn array of floats representing the 3-dimensional variable data.
varnameA string containing the name of the variable being written.
precisionAn integer specifying the precision of compression (for certain compression methods).
toleranceA double specifying the tolerance for compression (for certain compression methods).

The function performs the following steps:

  • Allocates memory for a temporary buffer to hold the variable data.
  • Copies the variable data from the 3-dimensional array var to the temporary buffer help.
  • Writes the variable data to the binary file specified by out using the specified compression method (uncompressed, packed, zfp, zstd, cmultiscale).
  • Logs a message indicating the successful writing of the variable data.
  • Frees the allocated memory.
Note
This function is typically used to write level data or other 3-dimensional meteorological variables to a binary file.
Depending on the value of ctl->met_type, the function writes the variable data using different compression methods. If ctl->met_type is not supported, an error message is logged.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9838 of file mptrac.c.

9845 {
9846
9847 float *help;
9848
9849 /* Allocate... */
9850 ALLOC(help, float,
9851 EX * EY * EP);
9852
9853 /* Copy data... */
9854#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) collapse(2)
9855 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
9856 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
9857 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
9858 help[ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, met->ny, ip, met->np)] = var[ix][iy][ip];
9859
9860 /* Write uncompressed data... */
9861 if (ctl->met_type == 1) {
9862 LOG(2, "Write 3-D variable: %s (uncompressed)", varname);
9863 FWRITE(help, float,
9864 (size_t) (met->nx * met->ny * met->np),
9865 out);
9866 }
9867
9868 /* Write packed data... */
9869 else if (ctl->met_type == 2)
9870 compress_pck(varname, help, (size_t) (met->ny * met->nx),
9871 (size_t) met->np, 0, out);
9872
9873 /* Write zfp data... */
9874#ifdef ZFP
9875 else if (ctl->met_type == 3) {
9876 FWRITE(&precision, int,
9877 1,
9878 out);
9879 FWRITE(&tolerance, double,
9880 1,
9881 out);
9882 compress_zfp(varname, help, met->np, met->ny, met->nx, precision,
9883 tolerance, 0, out);
9884 }
9885#endif
9886
9887 /* Write zstd data... */
9888#ifdef ZSTD
9889 else if (ctl->met_type == 4)
9890 compress_zstd(varname, help, (size_t) (met->np * met->ny * met->nx), 0,
9891 out);
9892#endif
9893
9894 /* Write cmultiscale data... */
9895#ifdef CMS
9896 else if (ctl->met_type == 5) {
9897 compress_cms(ctl, varname, help, (size_t) met->nx, (size_t) met->ny,
9898 (size_t) met->np, 0, out);
9899 }
9900#endif
9901
9902 /* Unknown method... */
9903 else {
9904 ERRMSG("MET_TYPE not supported!");
9905 LOG(3, "%d %g", precision, tolerance);
9906 }
9907
9908 /* Free... */
9909 free(help);
9910}

◆ write_met_nc_2d()

void write_met_nc_2d ( int  ncid,
const char *  varname,
met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY],
float  scl 
)

Writes a 2D meteorological variable to a NetCDF file.

This function writes a 2D meteorological variable, stored in the array var, to a NetCDF file with the specified variable name. The data is scaled by a factor scl before being written. The function handles memory allocation for the data copy, scaling, and freeing the allocated memory after writing the data to the NetCDF file.

Parameters
ncidThe NetCDF file ID. This is an integer that identifies the NetCDF file where the data will be written. It is assumed that this file has already been opened for writing.
varnameA pointer to a string containing the name of the variable in the NetCDF file where the data will be stored.
metA pointer to a structure of type met_t that contains metadata about the meteorological field, including the dimensions nx (number of points in x-direction) and ny (number of points in y-direction).
varA 2D array of dimensions EX x EY containing the meteorological data to be written. The data is provided in the format var[ix][iy], where ix is the index in the x-direction and iy is the index in the y-direction.
sclA scaling factor applied to each element in the var array before writing to the NetCDF file.
Returns
void This function does not return any value.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9914 of file mptrac.c.

9919 {
9920
9921 int varid;
9922 size_t start[4], count[4];
9923
9924 /* Allocate... */
9925 float *help;
9926 ALLOC(help, float,
9927 EX * EY);
9928
9929 /* Copy data... */
9930 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
9931 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
9932 help[ARRAY_2D(iy, ix, met->nx)] = scl * var[ix][iy];
9933
9934 /* Write data... */
9935 NC_PUT_FLOAT(varname, help, 0);
9936
9937 /* Free... */
9938 free(help);
9939}

◆ write_met_nc_3d()

void write_met_nc_3d ( int  ncid,
const char *  varname,
met_t *  met,
float  var[EX][EY][EP],
float  scl 
)

Writes a 3D meteorological variable to a NetCDF file.

This function writes a 3D meteorological variable, stored in the array var, to a NetCDF file with the specified variable name. The data is scaled by a factor scl before being written. The function handles memory allocation for the data copy, scaling, and freeing the allocated memory after writing the data to the NetCDF file.

Parameters
ncidThe NetCDF file ID. This is an integer that identifies the NetCDF file where the data will be written. It is assumed that this file has already been opened for writing.
varnameA pointer to a string containing the name of the variable in the NetCDF file where the data will be stored.
metA pointer to a structure of type met_t that contains metadata about the meteorological field, including the dimensions nx (number of points in the x-direction), ny (number of points in the y-direction), and np (number of points in the third dimension, e.g., pressure levels).
varA 3D array of dimensions EX x EY x EP containing the meteorological data to be written. The data is provided in the format var[ix][iy][ip], where ix is the index in the x-direction, iy is the index in the y-direction, and ip is the index in the third dimension (e.g., vertical levels).
sclA scaling factor applied to each element in the var array before writing to the NetCDF file.
Returns
void This function does not return any value.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9943 of file mptrac.c.

9949
9950 int varid;

9952
9953 /* Allocate... */
9954 float *help;
9955 ALLOC(help, float,
9956 EX * EY * EP);
9957
9958 /* Copy data... */
9959 for (int ix = 0; ix < met->nx; ix++)
9960 for (int iy = 0; iy < met->ny; iy++)
9961 for (int ip = 0; ip < met->np; ip++)
9962 help[ARRAY_3D(ip, iy, met->ny, ix, met->nx)] = scl * var[ix][iy][ip];
9963
9964 /* Write data... */
9965 NC_PUT_FLOAT(varname, help, 0);
9966
9967 /* Free... */
9968 free(help);
9969}

◆ write_output()

void write_output ( const char *  dirname,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met0,
met_t *  met1,
atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes various types of output data to files in a specified directory.

The write_output function writes various types of output data to files in the directory specified by the dirname parameter. The function takes control parameters (ctl), two meteorological data structures (met0 and met1), an atmospheric data structure (atm), and a time value (t) as input.

Parameters
dirnameA string representing the directory path where output files will be written.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
met0A pointer to a met_t structure representing the first set of meteorological data.
met1A pointer to a met_t structure representing the second set of meteorological data.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure representing atmospheric data.
tA double value representing the time at which the output is being written.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Parses the input time (t) to extract year, month, day, hour, minute, and second.
  • Updates host memory if necessary based on control parameters.
  • Writes atmospheric data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes gridded data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes CSI (Critical Success Index) data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes ensemble data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes profile data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes sample data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes station data to files if specified by control parameters.
  • Writes VTK (Visualization Toolkit) data to files if specified by control parameters.
Note
This function orchestrates the writing of various types of output data to files based on control parameters and the current simulation time.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 9973 of file mptrac.c.

9979 {
9980
9981 char ext[10], filename[2 * LEN];
9982
9983 double r;
9984
9985 int year, mon, day, hour, min, sec;
9986
9987 /* Get time... */
9988 jsec2time(t, &year, &mon, &day, &hour, &min, &sec, &r);
9989
9990 /* Update host... */
9991#ifdef _OPENACC
9992 if ((ctl->atm_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->atm_dt_out) == 0)
9993 || (ctl->grid_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->grid_dt_out) == 0)
9994 || (ctl->ens_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->ens_dt_out) == 0)
9995 || ctl->csi_basename[0] != '-' || ctl->prof_basename[0] != '-'
9996 || ctl->sample_basename[0] != '-' || ctl->stat_basename[0] != '-'
9997 || (ctl->vtk_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->vtk_dt_out) == 0)) {
9998 SELECT_TIMER("UPDATE_HOST", "MEMORY", NVTX_D2H);
9999#pragma acc update host(atm[:1])
10000 }
10001#endif
10002
10003 /* Write atmospheric data... */
10004 if (ctl->atm_basename[0] != '-' &&
10005 (fmod(t, ctl->atm_dt_out) == 0 || t == ctl->t_stop)) {
10006 if (ctl->atm_type_out == 0)
10007 sprintf(ext, "tab");
10008 else if (ctl->atm_type_out == 1)
10009 sprintf(ext, "bin");
10010 else if (ctl->atm_type_out == 2)
10011 sprintf(ext, "nc");
10012 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s_%04d_%02d_%02d_%02d_%02d.%s",
10013 dirname, ctl->atm_basename, year, mon, day, hour, min, ext);
10014 write_atm(filename, ctl, atm, t);
10015 }
10016
10017 /* Write gridded data... */
10018 if (ctl->grid_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->grid_dt_out) == 0) {
10019 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s_%04d_%02d_%02d_%02d_%02d.%s",
10020 dirname, ctl->grid_basename, year, mon, day, hour, min,
10021 ctl->grid_type == 0 ? "tab" : "nc");
10022 write_grid(filename, ctl, met0, met1, atm, t);
10023 }
10024
10025 /* Write CSI data... */
10026 if (ctl->csi_basename[0] != '-') {
10027 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s.tab", dirname, ctl->csi_basename);
10028 write_csi(filename, ctl, atm, t);
10029 }
10030
10031 /* Write ensemble data... */
10032 if (ctl->ens_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->ens_dt_out) == 0) {
10033 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s_%04d_%02d_%02d_%02d_%02d.tab",
10034 dirname, ctl->ens_basename, year, mon, day, hour, min);
10035 write_ens(filename, ctl, atm, t);
10036 }
10037
10038 /* Write profile data... */
10039 if (ctl->prof_basename[0] != '-') {
10040 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s.tab", dirname, ctl->prof_basename);
10041 write_prof(filename, ctl, met0, met1, atm, t);
10042 }
10043
10044 /* Write sample data... */
10045 if (ctl->sample_basename[0] != '-') {
10046 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s.tab", dirname, ctl->sample_basename);
10047 write_sample(filename, ctl, met0, met1, atm, t);
10048 }
10049
10050 /* Write station data... */
10051 if (ctl->stat_basename[0] != '-') {
10052 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s.tab", dirname, ctl->stat_basename);
10053 write_station(filename, ctl, atm, t);
10054 }
10055
10056 /* Write VTK data... */
10057 if (ctl->vtk_basename[0] != '-' && fmod(t, ctl->vtk_dt_out) == 0) {
10058 static int nvtk;
10059 if (t == ctl->t_start)
10060 nvtk = 0;
10061 sprintf(filename, "%s/%s_%05d.vtk", dirname, ctl->vtk_basename, ++nvtk);
10062 write_vtk(filename, ctl, atm, t);
10063 }
10064}

◆ write_prof()

void write_prof ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met0,
met_t *  met1,
const atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes profile data to a specified file.

The write_prof function writes profile data to a file specified by the filename parameter. It takes control parameters (ctl), two meteorological data structures (met0 and met1), an atmospheric data structure (atm), and a time value (t) as input.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the filename where the profile data will be written.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
met0A pointer to a met_t structure representing the first set of meteorological data.
met1A pointer to a met_t structure representing the second set of meteorological data.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure representing atmospheric data.
tA double value representing the time at which the profile data is being written.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Initializes variables and allocates memory if it's the start of the simulation.
  • Reads observation data and creates a new output file if necessary.
  • Writes header information to the output file.
  • Sets grid box size and vertical coordinates.
  • Processes observations and model data within the specified time interval.
  • Calculates and writes output data for each grid cell.
  • Finalizes by closing the output file and freeing allocated memory if it's the end of the simulation.
Note
This function writes profile data to a file, including time, altitude, coordinates, atmospheric properties, observed data, and the number of observations.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 10068 of file mptrac.c.

10074 {
10075
10076 static FILE *out;
10077
10078 static double *mass, *obsmean, *rt, *rz, *rlon, *rlat, *robs, *area,
10079 dz, dlon, dlat, *lon, *lat, *z, *press, temp, vmr, h2o, o3;
10080
10081 static int nobs, *obscount, ip, okay;
10082
10083 /* Set timer... */
10084 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_PROF", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
10085
10086 /* Init... */
10087 if (t == ctl->t_start) {
10088
10089 /* Check quantity index for mass... */
10090 if (ctl->qnt_m < 0)
10091 ERRMSG("Need quantity mass!");
10092
10093 /* Check molar mass... */
10094 if (ctl->molmass <= 0)
10095 ERRMSG("Specify molar mass!");
10096
10097 /* Allocate... */
10098 ALLOC(lon, double,
10099 ctl->prof_nx);
10100 ALLOC(lat, double,
10101 ctl->prof_ny);
10102 ALLOC(area, double,
10103 ctl->prof_ny);
10104 ALLOC(z, double,
10105 ctl->prof_nz);
10106 ALLOC(press, double,
10107 ctl->prof_nz);
10108 ALLOC(rt, double,
10109 NOBS);
10110 ALLOC(rz, double,
10111 NOBS);
10112 ALLOC(rlon, double,
10113 NOBS);
10114 ALLOC(rlat, double,
10115 NOBS);
10116 ALLOC(robs, double,
10117 NOBS);
10118
10119 /* Read observation data... */
10120 read_obs(ctl->prof_obsfile, ctl, rt, rz, rlon, rlat, robs, &nobs);
10121
10122 /* Create new output file... */
10123 LOG(1, "Write profile data: %s", filename);
10124 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
10125 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
10126
10127 /* Write header... */
10128 fprintf(out,
10129 "# $1 = time [s]\n"
10130 "# $2 = altitude [km]\n"
10131 "# $3 = longitude [deg]\n"
10132 "# $4 = latitude [deg]\n"
10133 "# $5 = pressure [hPa]\n"
10134 "# $6 = temperature [K]\n"
10135 "# $7 = volume mixing ratio [ppv]\n"
10136 "# $8 = H2O volume mixing ratio [ppv]\n"
10137 "# $9 = O3 volume mixing ratio [ppv]\n"
10138 "# $10 = observed BT index [K]\n"
10139 "# $11 = number of observations\n");
10140
10141 /* Set grid box size... */
10142 dz = (ctl->prof_z1 - ctl->prof_z0) / ctl->prof_nz;
10143 dlon = (ctl->prof_lon1 - ctl->prof_lon0) / ctl->prof_nx;
10144 dlat = (ctl->prof_lat1 - ctl->prof_lat0) / ctl->prof_ny;
10145
10146 /* Set vertical coordinates... */
10147 for (int iz = 0; iz < ctl->prof_nz; iz++) {
10148 z[iz] = ctl->prof_z0 + dz * (iz + 0.5);
10149 press[iz] = P(z[iz]);
10150 }
10151
10152 /* Set horizontal coordinates... */
10153 for (int ix = 0; ix < ctl->prof_nx; ix++)
10154 lon[ix] = ctl->prof_lon0 + dlon * (ix + 0.5);
10155 for (int iy = 0; iy < ctl->prof_ny; iy++) {
10156 lat[iy] = ctl->prof_lat0 + dlat * (iy + 0.5);
10157 area[iy] = dlat * dlon * SQR(RE * M_PI / 180.) * cos(DEG2RAD(lat[iy]));
10158 }
10159 }
10160
10161 /* Set time interval... */
10162 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10163 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10164
10165 /* Allocate... */
10166 ALLOC(mass, double,
10167 ctl->prof_nx * ctl->prof_ny * ctl->prof_nz);
10168 ALLOC(obsmean, double,
10169 ctl->prof_nx * ctl->prof_ny);
10170 ALLOC(obscount, int,
10171 ctl->prof_nx * ctl->prof_ny);
10172
10173 /* Loop over observations... */
10174 for (int i = 0; i < nobs; i++) {
10175
10176 /* Check time... */
10177 if (rt[i] < t0)
10178 continue;
10179 else if (rt[i] >= t1)
10180 break;
10181
10182 /* Check observation data... */
10183 if (!isfinite(robs[i]))
10184 continue;
10185
10186 /* Calculate indices... */
10187 int ix = (int) ((rlon[i] - ctl->prof_lon0) / dlon);
10188 int iy = (int) ((rlat[i] - ctl->prof_lat0) / dlat);
10189
10190 /* Check indices... */
10191 if (ix < 0 || ix >= ctl->prof_nx || iy < 0 || iy >= ctl->prof_ny)
10192 continue;
10193
10194 /* Get mean observation index... */
10195 int idx = ARRAY_2D(ix, iy, ctl->prof_ny);
10196 obsmean[idx] += robs[i];
10197 obscount[idx]++;
10198 }
10199
10200 /* Analyze model data... */
10201 for (ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++) {
10202
10203 /* Check time... */
10204 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10205 continue;
10206
10207 /* Get indices... */
10208 int ix = (int) ((atm->lon[ip] - ctl->prof_lon0) / dlon);
10209 int iy = (int) ((atm->lat[ip] - ctl->prof_lat0) / dlat);
10210 int iz = (int) ((Z(atm->p[ip]) - ctl->prof_z0) / dz);
10211
10212 /* Check indices... */
10213 if (ix < 0 || ix >= ctl->prof_nx ||
10214 iy < 0 || iy >= ctl->prof_ny || iz < 0 || iz >= ctl->prof_nz)
10215 continue;
10216
10217 /* Get total mass in grid cell... */
10218 int idx = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->prof_ny, iz, ctl->prof_nz);
10219 mass[idx] += atm->q[ctl->qnt_m][ip];
10220 }
10221
10222 /* Extract profiles... */
10223 for (int ix = 0; ix < ctl->prof_nx; ix++)
10224 for (int iy = 0; iy < ctl->prof_ny; iy++) {
10225 int idx2 = ARRAY_2D(ix, iy, ctl->prof_ny);
10226 if (obscount[idx2] > 0) {
10227
10228 /* Check profile... */
10229 okay = 0;
10230 for (int iz = 0; iz < ctl->prof_nz; iz++) {
10231 int idx3 = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->prof_ny, iz, ctl->prof_nz);
10232 if (mass[idx3] > 0) {
10233 okay = 1;
10234 break;
10235 }
10236 }
10237 if (!okay)
10238 continue;
10239
10240 /* Write output... */
10241 fprintf(out, "\n");
10242
10243 /* Loop over altitudes... */
10244 for (int iz = 0; iz < ctl->prof_nz; iz++) {
10245
10246 /* Get temperature, water vapor, and ozone... */
10247 INTPOL_INIT;
10248 intpol_met_time_3d(met0, met0->t, met1, met1->t, t, press[iz],
10249 lon[ix], lat[iy], &temp, ci, cw, 1);
10250 intpol_met_time_3d(met0, met0->h2o, met1, met1->h2o, t, press[iz],
10251 lon[ix], lat[iy], &h2o, ci, cw, 0);
10252 intpol_met_time_3d(met0, met0->o3, met1, met1->o3, t, press[iz],
10253 lon[ix], lat[iy], &o3, ci, cw, 0);
10254
10255 /* Calculate volume mixing ratio... */
10256 const int idx3 = ARRAY_3D(ix, iy, ctl->prof_ny, iz, ctl->prof_nz);
10257 vmr = MA / ctl->molmass * mass[idx3]
10258 / (RHO(press[iz], temp) * area[iy] * dz * 1e9);
10259
10260 /* Write output... */
10261 fprintf(out, "%.2f %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %g %d\n",
10262 t, z[iz], lon[ix], lat[iy], press[iz], temp, vmr, h2o, o3,
10263 obsmean[idx2] / obscount[idx2], obscount[idx2]);
10264 }
10265 }
10266 }
10267
10268 /* Free... */
10269 free(mass);
10270 free(obsmean);
10271 free(obscount);
10272
10273 /* Finalize... */
10274 if (t == ctl->t_stop) {
10275
10276 /* Close output file... */
10277 fclose(out);
10278
10279 /* Free... */
10280 free(lon);
10281 free(lat);
10282 free(area);
10283 free(z);
10284 free(press);
10285 free(rt);
10286 free(rz);
10287 free(rlon);
10288 free(rlat);
10289 free(robs);
10290 }
10291}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ write_sample()

void write_sample ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
met_t *  met0,
met_t *  met1,
const atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes sample data to a specified file.

The write_sample function writes sample data to a file specified by the filename parameter. It takes control parameters (ctl), two meteorological data structures (met0 and met1), an atmospheric data structure (atm), and a time value (t) as input.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the filename where the sample data will be written.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
met0A pointer to a met_t structure representing the first set of meteorological data.
met1A pointer to a met_t structure representing the second set of meteorological data.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure representing atmospheric data.
tA double value representing the time at which the sample data is being written.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Initializes variables and allocates memory if it's the start of the simulation.
  • Reads observation data and kernel data if necessary.
  • Creates a new output file and writes header information to it.
  • Sets latitude range, squared radius, and area.
  • Processes observations and calculates sample data within the specified time interval.
  • Writes output data for each observation.
  • Finalizes by closing the output file and freeing allocated memory if it's the end of the simulation.
Note
This function writes sample data to a file, including time, altitude, coordinates, surface area, layer depth, number of particles, column density, volume mixing ratio, and observed data.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 10295 of file mptrac.c.

10301 {
10302
10303 static FILE *out;
10304
10305 static double area, dlat, rmax2, *rt, *rz, *rlon, *rlat, *robs, kz[EP],
10306 kw[EP];
10307
10308 static int nobs, nk;
10309
10310 /* Set timer... */
10311 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_SAMPLE", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
10312
10313 /* Init... */
10314 if (t == ctl->t_start) {
10315
10316 /* Allocate... */
10317 ALLOC(rt, double,
10318 NOBS);
10319 ALLOC(rz, double,
10320 NOBS);
10321 ALLOC(rlon, double,
10322 NOBS);
10323 ALLOC(rlat, double,
10324 NOBS);
10325 ALLOC(robs, double,
10326 NOBS);
10327
10328 /* Read observation data... */
10329 read_obs(ctl->sample_obsfile, ctl, rt, rz, rlon, rlat, robs, &nobs);
10330
10331 /* Read kernel data... */
10332 if (ctl->sample_kernel[0] != '-')
10333 read_kernel(ctl->sample_kernel, kz, kw, &nk);
10334
10335 /* Create output file... */
10336 LOG(1, "Write sample data: %s", filename);
10337 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
10338 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
10339
10340 /* Write header... */
10341 fprintf(out,
10342 "# $1 = time [s]\n"
10343 "# $2 = altitude [km]\n"
10344 "# $3 = longitude [deg]\n"
10345 "# $4 = latitude [deg]\n"
10346 "# $5 = surface area [km^2]\n"
10347 "# $6 = layer depth [km]\n"
10348 "# $7 = number of particles [1]\n"
10349 "# $8 = column density [kg/m^2]\n"
10350 "# $9 = volume mixing ratio [ppv]\n"
10351 "# $10 = observed BT index [K]\n\n");
10352
10353 /* Set latitude range, squared radius, and area... */
10354 dlat = DY2DEG(ctl->sample_dx);
10355 rmax2 = SQR(ctl->sample_dx);
10356 area = M_PI * rmax2;
10357 }
10358
10359 /* Set time interval for output... */
10360 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10361 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10362
10363 /* Loop over observations... */
10364 for (int i = 0; i < nobs; i++) {
10365
10366 /* Check time... */
10367 if (rt[i] < t0)
10368 continue;
10369 else if (rt[i] >= t1)
10370 break;
10371
10372 /* Calculate Cartesian coordinates... */
10373 double x0[3];
10374 geo2cart(0, rlon[i], rlat[i], x0);
10375
10376 /* Set pressure range... */
10377 const double rp = P(rz[i]);
10378 double ptop = P(rz[i] + ctl->sample_dz);
10379 double pbot = P(rz[i] - ctl->sample_dz);
10380
10381 /* Init... */
10382 double mass = 0;
10383 int np = 0;
10384
10385 /* Loop over air parcels... */
10386#pragma omp parallel for default(shared) reduction(+:mass,np)
10387 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++) {
10388
10389 /* Check time... */
10390 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10391 continue;
10392
10393 /* Check latitude... */
10394 if (fabs(rlat[i] - atm->lat[ip]) > dlat)
10395 continue;
10396
10397 /* Check horizontal distance... */
10398 double x1[3];
10399 geo2cart(0, atm->lon[ip], atm->lat[ip], x1);
10400 if (DIST2(x0, x1) > rmax2)
10401 continue;
10402
10403 /* Check pressure... */
10404 if (ctl->sample_dz > 0)
10405 if (atm->p[ip] > pbot || atm->p[ip] < ptop)
10406 continue;
10407
10408 /* Add mass... */
10409 if (ctl->qnt_m >= 0)
10410 mass +=
10411 kernel_weight(kz, kw, nk, atm->p[ip]) * atm->q[ctl->qnt_m][ip];
10412 np++;
10413 }
10414
10415 /* Calculate column density... */
10416 const double cd = mass / (1e6 * area);
10417
10418 /* Calculate volume mixing ratio... */
10419 double vmr = 0;
10420 if (ctl->molmass > 0 && ctl->sample_dz > 0) {
10421 if (mass > 0) {
10422
10423 /* Get temperature... */
10424 double temp;
10425 INTPOL_INIT;
10426 intpol_met_time_3d(met0, met0->t, met1, met1->t, rt[i], rp,
10427 rlon[i], rlat[i], &temp, ci, cw, 1);
10428
10429 /* Calculate volume mixing ratio... */
10430 vmr = MA / ctl->molmass * mass
10431 / (RHO(rp, temp) * 1e6 * area * 1e3 * ctl->sample_dz);
10432 }
10433 } else
10434 vmr = NAN;
10435
10436 /* Write output... */
10437 fprintf(out, "%.2f %g %g %g %g %g %d %g %g %g\n", rt[i], rz[i],
10438 rlon[i], rlat[i], area, ctl->sample_dz, np, cd, vmr, robs[i]);
10439 }
10440
10441 /* Finalize...... */
10442 if (t == ctl->t_stop) {
10443
10444 /* Close output file... */
10445 fclose(out);
10446
10447 /* Free... */
10448 free(rt);
10449 free(rz);
10450 free(rlon);
10451 free(rlat);
10452 free(robs);
10453 }
10454}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ write_station()

void write_station ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes station data to a specified file.

The write_station function writes station data to a file specified by the filename parameter. It takes control parameters (ctl), an atmospheric data structure (atm), and a time value (t) as input.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the filename where the station data will be written.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure representing atmospheric data.
tA double value representing the time at which the station data is being written.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Initializes variables and opens a new file if it's the start of the simulation.
  • Writes header information to the output file.
  • Sets geolocation and search radius for station data.
  • Processes air parcels and writes station data within the specified time interval and search radius.
  • Writes station data for each air parcel satisfying the criteria.
  • Closes the output file if it's the end of the simulation.
Note
This function writes station data to a file, including time, altitude, longitude, latitude, and additional quantities specified in the control parameters.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 10458 of file mptrac.c.

10462 {
10463
10464 static FILE *out;
10465
10466 static double rmax2, x0[3], x1[3];
10467
10468 /* Set timer... */
10469 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_STATION", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
10470
10471 /* Init... */
10472 if (t == ctl->t_start) {
10473
10474 /* Write info... */
10475 LOG(1, "Write station data: %s", filename);
10476
10477 /* Create new file... */
10478 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
10479 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
10480
10481 /* Write header... */
10482 fprintf(out,
10483 "# $1 = time [s]\n"
10484 "# $2 = altitude [km]\n"
10485 "# $3 = longitude [deg]\n" "# $4 = latitude [deg]\n");
10486 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++)
10487 fprintf(out, "# $%i = %s [%s]\n", (iq + 5),
10488 ctl->qnt_name[iq], ctl->qnt_unit[iq]);
10489 fprintf(out, "\n");
10490
10491 /* Set geolocation and search radius... */
10492 geo2cart(0, ctl->stat_lon, ctl->stat_lat, x0);
10493 rmax2 = SQR(ctl->stat_r);
10494 }
10495
10496 /* Set time interval for output... */
10497 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10498 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10499
10500 /* Loop over air parcels... */
10501 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip++) {
10502
10503 /* Check time... */
10504 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10505 continue;
10506
10507 /* Check time range for station output... */
10508 if (atm->time[ip] < ctl->stat_t0 || atm->time[ip] > ctl->stat_t1)
10509 continue;
10510
10511 /* Check station flag... */
10512 if (ctl->qnt_stat >= 0)
10513 if ((int) atm->q[ctl->qnt_stat][ip])
10514 continue;
10515
10516 /* Get Cartesian coordinates... */
10517 geo2cart(0, atm->lon[ip], atm->lat[ip], x1);
10518
10519 /* Check horizontal distance... */
10520 if (DIST2(x0, x1) > rmax2)
10521 continue;
10522
10523 /* Set station flag... */
10524 if (ctl->qnt_stat >= 0)
10525 atm->q[ctl->qnt_stat][ip] = 1;
10526
10527 /* Write data... */
10528 fprintf(out, "%.2f %g %g %g",
10529 atm->time[ip], Z(atm->p[ip]), atm->lon[ip], atm->lat[ip]);
10530 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
10531 fprintf(out, " ");
10532 fprintf(out, ctl->qnt_format[iq], atm->q[iq][ip]);
10533 }
10534 fprintf(out, "\n");
10535 }
10536
10537 /* Close file... */
10538 if (t == ctl->t_stop)
10539 fclose(out);
10540}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ write_vtk()

void write_vtk ( const char *  filename,
const ctl_t *  ctl,
const atm_t *  atm,
const double  t 
)

Writes VTK (Visualization Toolkit) data to a specified file.

The write_vtk function writes VTK data to a file specified by the filename parameter. It takes control parameters (ctl), an atmospheric data structure (atm), and a time value (t) as input.

Parameters
filenameA string representing the filename where the VTK data will be written.
ctlA pointer to a ctl_t structure containing control parameters.
atmA pointer to an atm_t structure representing atmospheric data.
tA double value representing the time at which the VTK data is being written.

The function performs the following steps:

  • Sets a timer and logs information about writing VTK data.
  • Sets a time interval for output based on the specified time and control parameters.
  • Creates a new file and checks if the file creation was successful.
  • Counts the number of data points to be written.
  • Writes the VTK header, including metadata.
  • Writes point coordinates based on the sphere or Cartesian coordinate system.
  • Writes point data for each quantity specified in the control parameters.
  • Closes the output file.
Note
This function writes VTK data in ASCII format, including point coordinates and associated scalar data for visualization purposes.
Author
Lars Hoffmann

Definition at line 10544 of file mptrac.c.

10548 {
10549
10550 FILE *out;
10551
10552 /* Set timer... */
10553 SELECT_TIMER("WRITE_VTK", "OUTPUT", NVTX_WRITE);
10554
10555 /* Write info... */
10556 LOG(1, "Write VTK data: %s", filename);
10557
10558 /* Set time interval for output... */
10559 const double t0 = t - 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10560 const double t1 = t + 0.5 * ctl->dt_mod;
10561
10562 /* Create file... */
10563 if (!(out = fopen(filename, "w")))
10564 ERRMSG("Cannot create file!");
10565
10566 /* Count data points... */
10567 int np = 0;
10568 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip += ctl->vtk_stride) {
10569 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10570 continue;
10571 np++;
10572 }
10573
10574 /* Write header... */
10575 fprintf(out,
10576 "# vtk DataFile Version 3.0\n"
10577 "vtk output\n" "ASCII\n" "DATASET POLYDATA\n");
10578
10579 /* Write point coordinates... */
10580 fprintf(out, "POINTS %d float\n", np);
10581 if (ctl->vtk_sphere) {
10582 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip += ctl->vtk_stride) {
10583 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10584 continue;
10585 const double radius = (RE + Z(atm->p[ip]) * ctl->vtk_scale
10586 + ctl->vtk_offset) / RE;
10587 const double coslat = cos(DEG2RAD(atm->lat[ip]));
10588 const double x = radius * coslat * cos(DEG2RAD(atm->lon[ip]));
10589 const double y = radius * coslat * sin(DEG2RAD(atm->lon[ip]));
10590 const double z = radius * sin(DEG2RAD(atm->lat[ip]));
10591 fprintf(out, "%g %g %g\n", x, y, z);
10592 }
10593 } else
10594 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip += ctl->vtk_stride) {
10595 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10596 continue;
10597 fprintf(out, "%g %g %g\n", atm->lon[ip], atm->lat[ip],
10598 Z(atm->p[ip]) * ctl->vtk_scale + ctl->vtk_offset);
10599 }
10600
10601 /* Write point data... */
10602 fprintf(out, "POINT_DATA %d\n", np);
10603 for (int iq = 0; iq < ctl->nq; iq++) {
10604 fprintf(out, "SCALARS %s float 1\n" "LOOKUP_TABLE default\n",
10605 ctl->qnt_name[iq]);
10606 for (int ip = 0; ip < atm->np; ip += ctl->vtk_stride) {
10607 if (atm->time[ip] < t0 || atm->time[ip] > t1)
10608 continue;
10609 fprintf(out, "%g\n", atm->q[iq][ip]);
10610 }
10611 }
10612
10613 /* Close file... */
10614 fclose(out);
10615}
